  industrial-heavy vocab → left for review). This is correct behavior;
  preserving data integrity > automated coverage.
- **No bulk PATCH endpoint**. Single-row PATCH only. Bulk would risk
  systematic mislabeling on the wildcard bucket.

## Why not full reextract

P82 will fully reextract 335 MA towns with prompt v4. P83's recovery
gives us a clean dataset NOW (without waiting for the $80-120 P82 budget)
plus an independent guard against future regressions. After P82 ships,
the audit script will likely show `section_number=0` and significantly
reduced `wildcard_only` thanks to v4's hardened zone-subject prompt.

## File reference

- Migration: none (pure data + prompt + script changes; no schema)
- Audit: `scripts/audit_policy_rule_subject_loss.py`
- Tier 1 recovery: `scripts/recover_subject_section_number.py`
- Tier 2 recovery: `scripts/recover_subject_llm.py`
- Backend service: `plotmax/policy/service.py:list_subject_less_rules` +
  `_classify_subject_p83`
- Backend API: `plotmax/policy/api.py:list_subject_less_rules`
- Schema: `plotmax/policy/schemas.py:SubjectLessRuleRead`
- Sanitizer: `plotmax/policy/extractor.py:_sanitize_applicable_zones`
- Prompt patch: `plotmax/policy/prompts/v3/system.md` principles #12 + #13
- Frontend: `web/app/policy-review/subject-less/page.tsx`
- API client: `web/lib/api.ts` (`listSubjectLessRules`, `getTownZones`,
  `SubjectLessRule`, `ZoneDistrict`)
- Tests: `tests/unit/policy/test_p83_{audit,t1_recovery,t2_recovery,validator,subject_less_endpoint}.py`
  (54 tests total)

docs.title: P84 Full v4 Re-extract Runbook

versions.content:
# P84 Full v4 Re-extract Runbook

**Status:** ✅ shipped 2026-05-08 in v1.78.0
**Prompt version:** v4 (P82) + v4.1 patch (P85)
**Cohort delivered:** 311 MA towns (skipped 41 already-on-v4 from P82+P85 dry runs)
**Data state after run:** 301 jurisdictions on `prompt_version='v4'` `is_current=True`

---

## What this delivered

Re-ran the v4 LLM bylaw extractor across the full MA corpus through a new
**quality-gated extractor** (`plotmax/policy/quality_gated_extractor.py`):

- **Primary path:** Gemini 2.5 Pro (`gemini-2.5-pro`) — ~$0.10–0.30/town, fast
- **Fallback path:** Claude Sonnet 4.6 — ~3× cost, more reliable on
  prose-heavy bylaws (Cambridge / Somerville pattern, see P85)

Each town's primary run is auto-evaluated against a `QualityGate`
(rules ≥ 5 + zone_fk ≥ 0.3). On failure the primary `extraction_run` is
archived (`is_current=False`) and the Claude fallback runs against the
same source with new run id. Provenance (`primary_provider` /
`fallback_provider` / `fallback_reason` / `primary_metrics`) is stamped
into `extraction_metadata` for audit.

### Final shipped state

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total jurisdictions on v4 (is_current) | **301** |
| Gemini Pro primary_pass | 200 (66.4%) |
| Claude fallback_used | 101 (33.6%) |
| Total v4 policy_rule rows (is_current) | 27,261 |
| Mean rules/town | 86.1 |
| Median rules/town | 66 |
| Max rules in a single town (Kingston) | 436 |
| Mean zone_fk_rate | 0.55 |
| Median zone_fk_rate | 0.61 |
| Cost (LLM spend, full batch) | **$112.57** (cap: $200) |
| Errors mid-run (transient API + 6 unit-truncate) | 11 |
| Cohort skipped (already v4) | 4 (pilot dry runs) |

### Towns that errored mid-run (need re-run later)

Eleven towns failed inside the wrapper — primarily transient Anthropic
500s + cambridge_class window failures + 6 DataError rows from a
varchar-length edge case (defensive truncate now lives in
`BylawExtractor._insert_rule`). Re-running the batch idempotently picks
them up:

  - `amesbury`, `pittsfield` — cambridge_class window failures
  - `billerica`, `oxford`, `truro` — Anthropic API 500/RemoteDisconnected
  - `leverett`, `marlborough`, `nantucket`, `rutland`, `webster`,
    `wenham` — DataError on `unit` column (now defensively truncated;
    rerun will succeed)

---

## Files / artifacts

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `plotmax/policy/quality_gated_extractor.py` | Wrapper + gate evaluator |
| `scripts/extract_bylaw_v4_batch.py` | Async batch driver |
| `scripts/audit_v4_reextract_quality.py` | Read-only quality audit |
| `data/audits/p84_v4_batch_full_20260508_055514.json` | Per-town JSON dump (gitignored) |
| `tests/unit/policy/test_p84_quality_gate.py` | 11 unit tests |
| `tests/unit/policy/test_p84_settings.py` | 7 settings tests |

---

## How to run

### Pilot 5 (dry-run gate)

```bash
uv run python scripts/extract_bylaw_v4_batch.py \
    --cohort pilot5 \
    --concurrency 2 \
    --cost-ceiling 1.5 \
    --no-skip-done   # force rerun even if v4 exists
```

Pilot exits 1 if fewer than 4/5 towns succeed (primary_pass + fallback_used
both count as success — fallback is the design).

### Full cohort

```bash
uv run python scripts/extract_bylaw_v4_batch.py \
    --cohort full \
    --concurrency 3 \
    --cost-ceiling 1.5 \
    --total-cap 200 \
    --gate-zone-fk 0.3 \
    --skip-done
```

Idempotent. Skips towns already on v4 `is_current=True`. Cumulative cost
cap triggers graceful-stop after the in-flight town. JSON dump lands in
`data/audits/p84_v4_batch_full_<timestamp>.json` for post-mortem.

### Audit existing v4 quality

```bash
# Pretty table to stdout
uv run python scripts/audit_v4_reextract_quality.py

# Only flag low-quality towns (rules<5 OR zone_fk<0.3)
uv run python scripts/audit_v4_reextract_quality.py --low-only

# Machine-readable CSV
uv run python scripts/audit_v4_reextract_quality.py --csv > /tmp/v4_audit.csv
```

---

## Quality gate semantics

The gate (`plotmax/policy/quality_gated_extractor.py:_evaluate_gate`)
evaluates each primary run against:

| Check | Default threshold | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `rules_count` | ≥ 5 | Below this = total LLM abstain (Cambridge 0 pattern from P85) |
| `confidence_pass_rate` | ≥ 0.7 fraction with `confidence ≥ 0.7` | Skipped when ≥ 90% of rules report `confidence=None` (Gemini Pro schema bug — see "Known issues") |
| `zone_fk_rate` | ≥ 0.3 fraction with `zone_district_id` resolved | Catches Somerville section-number-leak (zone code never anchored) |

**Why 0.3 zone_fk floor instead of 0.7?** P84 pilot data showed Gemini Pro
zone_fk hovers 50–78% on the 5-city cohort (vs Claude 95%+). 0.7 would
fallback every Gemini run; 0.3 catches truly-broken extractions
(Cambridge 0% / Somerville 20%) while letting decent Gemini runs ship at
1/3 the Claude cost.

---

## Known issues / future work

### 1. Gemini Pro `confidence=None` schema bug (P86 idea)

Gemini Pro emits `confidence: float | None = None` as `null` 59/60 times
even when explicitly prompted. Workaround: gate skips confidence
enforcement when ≥ 90% of rules report None
(`metrics["confidence_none_rate"] >= 0.90`) and judges quality by
`zone_fk_rate` + `rules_count` only. P86 follow-up: investigate Gemini
schema serialization for optional float fields, and consider switching
to Claude-only or post-processing confidence inference.

### 2. Gemini error spikes (P86 follow-up)

GCP API console shows occasional 100% error spikes (Apr 23, May 7
historical) and 0.32% steady-state rate. Captured in
`data/audits/p86_gemini_error_observations_20260507.md`. P86 will add:

  - Pre-batch health probe (test 1 known-good town before launching N)
  - Exponential backoff + retry budget for `MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL` /
    quota errors
  - Audit-log error categorization (transient vs permanent)

### 3. 62 low-quality towns flagged

`scripts/audit_v4_reextract_quality.py --low-only` flags 62 towns where
rules < 5 OR zone_fk < 0.3. About half are short-bylaw towns (Hancock 1
rule with zone_fk=1.0 — actually fine), and half are Claude-fallback
runs that *also* couldn't anchor zones (suggesting their bylaws don't
follow standard zone-table structure). Triage in a follow-up P-item.

### 4. 33 jurisdictions not yet on v4

`policy_extraction_run.is_current=True` includes 33 non-v4 rows. These
are towns whose bylaw extraction either preceded P82's v4 prompt or
have no `crawl_artifact` with `sections>=10` (so the cohort filter
skipped them). Backfill is part of P30+ town registry work — not P84
scope.

---

## Rollback

If the v4 corpus needs to be reverted:

```sql
-- Mark all v4 runs as not-current
UPDATE policy_extraction_run
   SET is_current = FALSE
 WHERE prompt_version = 'v4';

UPDATE policy_rule
   SET is_current = FALSE
  FROM policy_extraction_run
 WHERE policy_rule.extraction_run_id = policy_extraction_run.id
   AND policy_extraction_run.prompt_version = 'v4';

-- Re-promote prior version (e.g. v3.1)
UPDATE policy_extraction_run
   SET is_current = TRUE
 WHERE prompt_version = 'v3.1';
```

Provenance `extraction_metadata` survives — primary/fallback decisions
are still auditable.

---

## Cost economics

Full batch: **$112.57 / 307 towns processed = $0.367/town avg**, of which:

- Gemini Pro primary alone (66.4% of towns): cheap (~$0.10–0.30/town)
- Claude fallback (33.6% of towns): ~3× cost (~$0.50–1.20/town)
- Avoiding pure-Claude across all 307 saved roughly **$200–250** vs the
  P82 single-provider plan.

Headline economics for future planning:

| Plan | Est. cost (~300 towns) |
|---|---|
| Pure Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $300–360 |
| Pure Gemini Pro (no fallback) | $40–60 — but ~30% would have <5 rules |
| **Quality-gated (delivered)** | **$112** + 27K usable rules |

docs.title: P85 — Cambridge / Somerville Fix Gate (v1.77.1)

versions.content:
# P85 — Cambridge / Somerville Fix Gate (v1.77.1)

> Forensic analysis + 4-commit fix chain for the Cambridge 0-rows +
> Somerville zone_FK 0% bugs surfaced in P82 5-city dry-run. Required
> as a gate BEFORE P84 (full 335-town v4 reextract) — without these fixes
> the bugs would be batch-amplified to all 335 towns.

## Three independent root causes (forensic)

P82 v4 dry-run produced these failure patterns:
- Cambridge: 0 rows extracted (cost $0.11, single LLM call returning empty)
- Somerville: 126 rules but zone_FK = 0% (every rule's zone code was a section number like "5.1" or "6.3")

Forensic analysis revealed three independent bugs:

### 1. zoning_districts pollution (P81 C2 backfill)

`policy_metadata_backfill` blindly inserted any `districts_found` value from
extraction_metadata into `zoning_districts.code`. Somerville's v3 extraction
had emitted `["4.1", "4.2", "4.3", "4.4", "5.1", "6.1"]` as districts_found,
which became 6 polluted rows in `zoning_districts`.

When v4's `_format_zone_vocab` injected the vocab block, those polluted
codes appeared — and the LLM dutifully echoed them back as zone codes,
which then got stripped by P83 sanitizer leaving applicable_zones empty.

**Scope:** Somerville only (1 town, 6 rows). Other MA towns clean.

### 2. extract_bylaw_v4 path bypassed cambridge_class dispatch

`scripts/extract_bylaw_v4.py:83` called `BylawExtractor.extract()` directly,
which had NO cambridge_class gate. Cambridge's 756K artifact was
section_locator-trimmed to 100K and sent as a single LLM call. Claude
saw a 100K-char prose excerpt without the dimensional table and emitted
zero rules.

The P82-era cambridge dispatch lived in `pipeline.run_full_pipeline`,
NOT `BylawExtractor.extract()` — so the v4 dry-run script bypassed it.

### 3. Prompt v4 force-strength insufficient

Even with vocab injection, the v4 system prompt let LLMs treat zone
vocabulary as a soft suggestion. Worse, when the bylaw structure was
ambiguous (e.g. Somerville's `# 4.1 MID-RISE 3 (MR3)` headers), the LLM
sometimes picked the section number rather than the parenthesized zone
code.

## Fix architecture (4 commits)

### C1 — pollution cleanup + defensive vocab sanitizer

- `scripts/audit_zoning_districts_pollution.py` — full-MA scan, exit 1 on
  any `^\d+\.\d+` pattern in code column. Found 6 rows in Somerville.
- `scripts/fix_somerville_zoning_districts_pollution.py` — surgical
  DELETE (idempotent, dry-run default). Audit: 6 → 0 rows. Zero
  `policy_rule.zone_district_id` referrers (FK constraint already
  ON DELETE SET NULL from P82 C1).
- `extractor._format_zone_vocab` — defensive filter: any vocab entry
  matching `^\d+\.\d+` is dropped before injection. Belt-and-suspenders
  guard against future re-pollution.

### C2 — cambridge_class dispatch in BylawExtractor.extract()

- New `_extract_cambridge_class()` private method on BylawExtractor —
  uses `window_splitter.split_text` for >400K artifacts, calls LLM per
  window, deduplicates rules + use_matrix_rows + unions
  districts/overlays/state_refs across windows, aggregates cost+tokens.
- `extract()` checks `art.processed_size_bytes > CAMBRIDGE_BYTES_LIMIT`
  BEFORE calling locator, so cambridge artifacts use
  `locate_sections_cambridge` (500K budget) and route through
  multi-window. Standard artifacts unchanged.

### C3 — prompt v4.1 patch + section-aware splitter

- `prompts/v4/system.md` Principle #14 REVISED to HARD requirement.
  Explicit reverse example: `# 4.1 MID-RISE 3 (MR3)` → zone is `MR3`,
  NOT `4.1`. Section numbers always go in `source_section`.
- Principle #15 NEW: every rule's `applicable_zones[]` MUST be a subset
  of `districts_found[]` (catches "I guessed a zone code" failure mode).
- `track_c_dimensional.md` adds Cambridge §5.30 Multifamily real-bylaw
  few-shot — 3×4 dimensional table → 8 dimensional rules + 1 use_matrix
  N entry.
- `window_splitter.split_text_section_aware` — searches for markdown
  headers within ±5K chars of stride point and snaps window boundary
  to header offset. Falls back to fixed stride when no nearby header.
- `_extract_cambridge_class` switched to use the section-aware splitter.

### C4 — defensive multi-window error handling + 5-city dry-run + docs

- `_extract_cambridge_class` catches per-window LLM failures (ValidationError /
  schema mismatches / API errors) and skips bad windows. Only raises
  if ALL windows fail. Acton previously failed entirely because a
  single window returned malformed JSON; now we lose only that window.
- `scripts/p85_forensic_compare.py` — 5-city dry-run + diff vs P82
  baseline JSON + acceptance gates with markdown report.

## 5-city dry-run results (v4 baseline → v4.1 fix)

| city | rules (v4 → v4.1) | zone_FK | use_FK | use_matrix | cost ($) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Nahant | — → **137** ✅ | 0% → **94.2%** ✅ | 0% → 41.6% | 0 → **272** | 1.14 |
| Acton | ERROR → fixed in C4 (defensive)\* | — | — | — | — |
| Cambridge | 0 → 0 ⚠️ | 0% → 0% | — | — | 0.55 |
| Newton | 116 → 112 (stable) | 96.6% → 95.5% | 22.4% → 16.1% | 13 → 18 | 0.47 |
| Somerville | 126 → **186** ✅ | 0% → **51.6%** ✅ | 98.4% → 67.7% | 17 → **37** | 1.53 |

\*Re-run after C4 ValidationError fix lands.

### Wins

1. **Nahant (multi-window now)**: 0% → **94.2% zone_FK**, **272 use_matrix
   rows** (was 0). Large bylaws now extract their full Use Schedule. This
   alone is a major v4.1 improvement.
2. **Somerville**: 0% → **51.6% zone_FK** improvement (51.6 percentage
   points). Pollution cleanup + prompt strengthening cut section-number
   emission dramatically, though some still leak (P84 Gemini Pro will
   address residual; see deferred section).
3. **Newton**: held within ±5pp of v4 baseline — no regression from new
   dispatch path.

### Known partial fixes — deferred to P84 Gemini Pro

1. **Cambridge 0 rows**: the bylaw is prose-heavy with dimensional content
   buried in long paragraphs. Claude consistently sees no extractable
   rules per window. Section-aware splitting alone doesn't help — the
   structural problem is that Cambridge's dimensional schedule is
   tabular text spanning ~30K chars within a 720K bylaw, and Claude
   under-attends to it. **P84 plan**: try Gemini Pro 2.5 (1M context,
   different attention bias) — may surface the table without splitter.
   If Gemini Pro also fails, prompt v5 with explicit Cambridge §5.30 cue
   as a few-shot is the next step.
2. **Somerville zone_FK 51.6% (target ≥70%)**: residual section-number
   emission persists despite prompt v4.1 #14/#15. Likely a model-level
   confusion specific to Somerville's `# 4.1 MID-RISE 3 (MR3)` heading
   format. **P84 plan**: Gemini Pro may handle this differently;
   alternatively a dedicated Somerville zone-extraction step (heading
   regex pre-pass) injected as additional vocab.

These deferrals are documented as P84 acceptance criteria; P85 ships the
infrastructure improvements that make Cambridge / Somerville analysis
tractable in the first place.

## Operating SOP

```bash
# 1. Audit pollution (whole MA)
uv run python scripts/audit_zoning_districts_pollution.py --state MA
# expected: 0 rows after C1 fix; exit 0

# 2. Forensic compare 5-city
uv run python scripts/p85_forensic_compare.py --provider claude --cost-ceiling 5.0

# 3. (After P84 ships Gemini Pro fallback) re-run with --provider gemini
uv run python scripts/p85_forensic_compare.py --provider gemini --cost-ceiling 1.0
```

## File reference

- Audit + fix scripts: `scripts/audit_zoning_districts_pollution.py`,
  `scripts/fix_somerville_zoning_districts_pollution.py`
- Forensic compare: `scripts/p85_forensic_compare.py`
- Extractor: `plotmax/policy/extractor.py` —
  `_format_zone_vocab` filter, `_extract_cambridge_class` (with
  per-window error handling), cambridge_class dispatch in `extract()`
- Splitter: `plotmax/policy/window_splitter.py` —
  `split_text_section_aware`, `_find_header_offsets`, `_HEADER_LINE_RE`
- Prompt patch: `plotmax/policy/prompts/v4/system.md` (#14 REVISED + #15
  NEW), `track_c_dimensional.md` (Cambridge §5.30 few-shot),
  `CHANGELOG.md` (v4.1 entry)
- Tests: `tests/unit/policy/test_p85_{pollution_fix,cambridge_dispatch,
  section_aware_splitter}.py` (37 tests)

## Acceptance verdict

**P85 ships as a gate-loosening progress release**:
- ✅ Schema infra works (pollution cleanup + cambridge dispatch + section-aware splitter)
- ✅ Nahant + Newton stable; Acton ValidationError defensively handled
- ⚠️ Cambridge + Somerville full fix deferred to P84 (Gemini Pro fallback +
  potential prompt v5)
- 🔓 P84 unblocked — full reextract can proceed using P85's repaired
  infrastructure, with P84 Gemini Pro fallback handling Cambridge
  + Somerville-class residual issues

docs.title: P87 Policy KG Frontend Visualization — Reference

versions.content:
# P87 Policy KG Frontend Visualization — Reference

**Status:** ✅ shipped 2026-05-08 in v1.80.0
**Module:** `web/components/policy/` + `plotmax/policy/` (annotation backend)
**Entry point:** `/jurisdictions/town/{id}` 政策分析 Card

---

## What this delivers

End-to-end UI for the Policy Knowledge Graph extraction outputs:

| Surface | Source data | Component |
|---|---|---|
| Use matrix grid (Zone × Use → P\|SP\|N\|A) | `zone_use_permission` (P81/P82) | `UseMatrixGrid` |
| Provenance ribbon (3 states) | `policy_extraction_run.extraction_metadata` (P84) | `ProvenanceRibbon` |
| Overlay toggle row | `zoning_districts.is_overlay=true` (P81) | `OverlayToggleRow` |
| Cell drill-in panel (L1+L2+L3+L4 bundle) | `/applicable-rules` (P81) | `CellDrilldownPanel` |
| Source / Evidence / Context / Analysis | `policy_rule.verbatim_quote` + crawl_artifact sections | `SourceContextBlock` |
| Annotation threads (governance + ML labels) | `policy_annotation` (new in P87 C4) | `AnnotationsPanel` |

---

## Provenance ribbon — 3 states

| State | Trigger | Color | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `primary_pass` | `prompt_version=v4` AND `extraction_metadata.fallback_provider=null` | 🟢 green | Gemini Pro succeeded the QualityGate |
| `fallback_used` | `prompt_version=v4` AND `extraction_metadata.fallback_provider!=null` | 🟠 amber | Claude Sonnet rescued (gate failed primary). Still high quality — fallback is by design — amber == "noteworthy", not "broken". |
| `v3_legacy` | `prompt_version != "v4"` | ⚪ gray | Pre-P82 extraction. UI nudges operator to trigger v4 reextract. |

---

## Cell drill-in flow

1. User opens `/jurisdictions/town/{id}` → 政策分析 Card.
2. Use matrix grid renders pivot of `zone_use_permission` rows.
3. Click any cell → `selectedZone` + `selectedUse` set; right panel
   queries `/applicable-rules?zone_code=...&use_code=...&overlay_codes=...&prompt_version=v4`.
4. Right panel shows L2 permission block + L3 dimensional rules
   (split into base / overlay-modified buckets via
   `groupRulesByOverlay`) + L4 state law / env constraints.
5. User clicks any rule's row → expands disclosure body containing
   `SourceContextBlock` (Source / Evidence / Context / Analysis lineage)
   and `AnnotationsPanel` (threaded comments + new-annotation form).
6. User can toggle overlays in the row above the grid → drill-in panel
   re-fetches (cache key includes sorted `overlay_codes.join(",")`).

---

## Annotation system

### Schema (`policy_annotation` table)

```
id                       UUID PK
target_kind              VARCHAR(40)  CHECK (policy_rule | zone_use_permission)
target_id                UUID
kind                     VARCHAR(40)  CHECK (note | flag | correction |
                                              label_correct | label_incorrect)
parent_id                UUID self-FK NULL  ← threading
author_id                UUID NULL FK admin_user
note_text                TEXT
payload                  JSONB
status                   VARCHAR(20)  CHECK (open | resolved | dismissed)
source_extraction_run_id UUID NOT NULL FK policy_extraction_run (RESTRICT)  ← provenance
created_at, updated_at, resolved_at, resolved_by
```

`source_extraction_run_id` is **NOT NULL** with `ON DELETE RESTRICT` —
every annotation is provenance-linked to the run it was made against,
and runs can't be hard-deleted while annotations reference them.
Re-extracting a town doesn't break annotations: they keep pointing to
the original (now `is_current=False`) run.

### Annotation kinds — when to use which

| kind | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `note` | Free-form reviewer comment (most common) |
| `flag` | "Looks suspicious — needs follow-up" — surface to dashboards |
| `correction` | Reviewer proposes a fix; **does NOT auto-apply** to the rule. To apply the fix, run a separate `PATCH /api/v1/policy/rules/{id}` (existing P57 endpoint). |
| `label_correct` | ML training signal: "this extraction is correct as-is" |
| `label_incorrect` | ML training signal: "this extraction is wrong" — pair with a `note` reply explaining what's wrong |

### Auth model

**No RBAC on annotations.** Any authenticated user can read / create /
patch any annotation. Rationale (P87 design decision): annotations
double as ML training data, and we want broad participation across
reviewers, domain experts, and external annotators. If a workflow ever
needs author-only edits or admin-only resolves, that's a follow-up
P-item.

### Threading

`parent_id` self-FK with `ON DELETE SET NULL`. Backend returns the flat
list sorted by `created_at ASC`; frontend builds the tree client-side
via `buildThread` (`web/components/policy/annotation-threading.ts`).
Replies render indented (16px per depth level).

### Exporting training data

Currently no dedicated export endpoint. SQL recipe:

```sql
-- All ML labels with their target rule data
SELECT
  ann.kind,
  ann.created_at,
  ann.note_text         AS reviewer_reasoning,
  ann.payload,
  pr.parameter,
  pr.parameter_subtype,
  pr.base_value,
  pr.unit,
  pr.value_text,
  pr.verbatim_quote,
  pr.confidence,
  per.prompt_version,
  per.extraction_metadata ->> 'primary_provider' AS provider
FROM policy_annotation ann
JOIN policy_rule pr ON ann.target_id = pr.id
JOIN policy_extraction_run per ON ann.source_extraction_run_id = per.id
WHERE ann.target_kind = 'policy_rule'
  AND ann.kind IN ('label_correct','label_incorrect')
  AND ann.status != 'dismissed'
ORDER BY ann.created_at;
```

Export to CSV / JSONL for SFT or RLHF training pairs.

---

## Source-context endpoint

`GET /api/v1/policy/rules/{rule_id}/source-context` returns the FULL
bylaw section text containing the rule's `verbatim_quote`, plus
`verbatim_offset` so the frontend can highlight the snippet within its
context window (±500 chars by default; "展开全文" button reveals the
entire section).

Lookup strategy (`get_rule_source_context` in `service.py`):

1. Read `policy_rule.crawl_artifact_id` → `crawl_artifact.storage_path`
2. Walk `data/{storage_path}/sections/*.md`
3. Try **exact substring match** of `verbatim_quote` first
4. If not found, fall back to **section-number prefix** match
   (compress the rule's `source_section` to alphanumeric → match against
   markdown filename)
5. If still not found, return `verbatim_offset = -1` + first 1000 chars
   of any matching file (frontend shows "verbatim 未定位" warning)

---

## Known boundaries

1. **Overlay "假设模式"** — toggling overlays on a town has no
   parcel-level resolution. A real parcel would intersect specific
   overlay polygons; here the user explicitly assumes which overlays are
   active. Full spatial overlay resolution lands in P30+.
2. **Use matrix sparseness** — 197 / 301 v4 cities have use_matrix data.
   The other ~104 ran v3 or hit an extraction path that skipped track C.
   Towns without data show an empty state with explanation.
3. **Sticky col + sticky header in Safari** — works on Safari 15+
   (current min). On older Safari falls back to plain scroll.
4. **No row-level ACL on annotations** — by design. Users can edit
   each other's annotations. If this becomes a problem, P-item it.
5. **Frontend test coverage** — only pure-fn helpers
   (`use-matrix-status`, `provenance-format`,
   `dimensional-rules-grouping`, `annotation-threading`) covered via
   `node --experimental-strip-types --test`. React component DOM tests
   would require introducing vitest + @testing-library/react — separate
   P-item if needed.

---

## Files

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `alembic/versions/z001_policy_annotation.py` | Polymorphic annotation table |
| `plotmax/policy/models.py` | `PolicyAnnotation` ORM |
| `plotmax/policy/schemas.py` | Pydantic models for annotation + source-context |
| `plotmax/policy/service.py` | `list_annotations` / `create_annotation` / `patch_annotation` / `get_rule_source_context` |
| `plotmax/policy/api.py` | 4 new endpoints (annotations CRUD + source-context) + `list_runs` jurisdiction_id filter |
| `web/lib/api.ts` | Type interfaces + 7 fetcher methods |
| `web/components/policy/use-matrix-grid.tsx` | Use matrix table |
| `web/components/policy/provenance-ribbon.tsx` | 3-state provenance ribbon |
| `web/components/policy/overlay-toggle-row.tsx` | Overlay assumption toggles |
| `web/components/policy/cell-drilldown-panel.tsx` | Right-side drill-in (L1+L2+L3+L4 bundle) |
| `web/components/policy/source-context-block.tsx` | Source / Evidence / Context / Analysis disclosure |
| `web/components/policy/annotations-panel.tsx` | Threaded annotation list + new-annotation form |
| `web/components/policy/policy-analysis-section.tsx` | Top-level integration card |
| `tests/unit/policy/test_p87_list_runs_filter.py` | Backend filter test (2 cases) |
| `tests/unit/policy/test_p87_annotations.py` | Annotation CRUD + threading + provenance + CHECK constraints (6 cases) |
| `web/components/policy/__tests__/*.test.mjs` | 4 helper test files (28 node:test cases) |

docs.title: P95 — Use-matrix master-table grounding fix

versions.content:
# P95 — Use-matrix master-table grounding fix

> **Status**: ✅ delivered v1.87.0 (2026-05-13)
> **Author**: P95 investigation thread (Danvers R-I × single_family_detached)

## TL;DR

LLM Track B (use matrix) was extracting `SP` for a cell whose true value
is `P` because the master `TABLE 1: TABLE OF ALLOWABLE USES` lived in a
section file the segmenter mis-named "Section XVII Highway Zone Corridor".
The locator never anchored Track B on the master table, so the LLM
defaulted to a Section 33 RCD subsection (which IS by special permit) and
emitted SP with 0.95 confidence.

Fix is three coordinated changes:

1. **section_locator.py** — body-pattern scan for `TABLE \d+: TABLE OF
   (ALLOWABLE) USES?` classifies the section as `use_schedule` regardless
   of title, and bumps it to priority −1 so it survives 100K budget pressure.
   `build_combined_input` emits a distinct `=== USE SCHEDULE MASTER TABLE ===`
   banner.
2. **prompt v4.2** (`track_b_use_matrix.md`) — new "Master-table grounding"
   section requires every `verbatim_quote` to come from the master-table row,
   never from RCD / cluster / overlay subsections.
3. **frontend P87 drill-in** (`cell-drilldown-panel.tsx`) — `PermissionBlock`
   now expands on click to show `verbatim_quote` / `conditions_text` /
   `confidence`; verbatim <50 chars is amber-flagged (matches audit threshold).

Plus operational tools:

- **`scripts/audit_use_matrix_fingerprint.py`** — scans for the failure
  fingerprint (`status='SP'` + `verbatim<50` + `conf>0.8` + non-master-table
  source). Prod MA snapshot: **833 suspect rows** before any v4.2 re-extract.
- **`scripts/fix_danvers_ri_single_family.py`** — single-row UPDATE that
  archives the original LLM output to `extraction_metadata.manual_corrections`
  before writing the correction.

## Root-cause walkthrough

### Symptom

```sql
SELECT status, source_section, verbatim_quote, conditions_text, confidence
FROM zone_use_permission
WHERE id = '43e1ffd8-60fc-4a8d-9824-b84ad60ddbe8';
```

Pre-fix:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| status | `SP` ← wrong |
| source_section | `"Section 33.2.A"` |
| verbatim_quote | `"Single family dwellings"` (23 chars) |
| conditions_text | `"Permitted in a Residential Conservation Development (RCD) by Special Permit."` |
| confidence | `0.95` |

Real value from the master TABLE 1 on Danvers bylaw page 117:

```
| Use                       | R-I | R-II | R-III | … |
| Dwelling - Single Family  |  P  |  P   |   P   | … |
```

### Why the LLM picked Section 33

Three failure layers, all needed to produce the bug:

1. **Segmenter mis-naming**. The Danvers PDF segmenter cut sections on the
   "Section XVII" heading at page 117. TABLE 1 starts on a later page in
   that same section file. So the file ends up named
   `section_xvii_highway_zone_corridor_a_4_p117.md` even though it contains
   the master use schedule.

2. **Locator title-only matching**. `section_locator._classify_section`
   pre-P95 checked only `title_lc` against `DIMENSIONAL_TITLE_TERMS`. The
   list includes `"table of"`, but title "Section XVII Highway Zone
   Corridor" doesn't match.

3. **Prompt has no master-table anchor**. v4.1 said "emit the most
   permissive status when a cell has multiple values" but didn't say
   "ground every status in the master table; never override from a
   subsection." When the LLM saw both:
   - master TABLE 1 (`P` for R-I × Dwelling - Single Family), and
   - §33.2.A "The Planning Board may grant a special permit for an RCD in
     RI… the following uses shall be permitted in an RCD: A. Single family
     dwellings"

   …§33 had a much higher textual density of `single_family_detached`-related
   tokens near `special permit`, so the LLM picked it.

### Why this is widespread

Audit fingerprint:

```bash
uv run python scripts/audit_use_matrix_fingerprint.py --state MA
```

Counts (pre-fix, 2026-05-13 prod):

| State | Suspect rows |
|---|---|
| MA | **833** |

The 833 are not all "wrong" — the fingerprint is a screening filter, not a
truth check. But the rate is high enough (≈ 833 / ~70K MA zone_use_permission
rows = ~1.2%) to justify v4.2 cohort backfill, which is staged as a separate
P-item.

## What landed in v1.87.0

### Code

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `plotmax/policy/section_locator.py` | `USE_TABLE_BODY_PATTERN`, `use_schedule` category, priority −1, banner. |
| `tests/unit/policy/test_section_locator_use_schedule.py` | 7 tests covering pattern, classification, budget priority, banner output, prose-mention negative. |
| `plotmax/policy/prompts/v4/track_b_use_matrix.md` | "Master-table grounding" rules + clarified prose-fallback branch. |
| `plotmax/policy/prompts/v4/CHANGELOG.md` | v4.2 entry referencing P95. |
| `web/components/policy/cell-drilldown-panel.tsx` | `PermissionBlock` expandable + amber-flagged short verbatim. |
| `scripts/audit_use_matrix_fingerprint.py` | Read-only audit (JSON / table output, --state, --limit). |
| `scripts/fix_danvers_ri_single_family.py` | Single-row UPDATE with snapshot to `extraction_metadata.manual_corrections`. |

### Data (prod)

- Danvers R-I × `residential.single_family_detached`: `SP` → `P` (audit
  trail in `policy_extraction_run.extraction_metadata.manual_corrections`,
  run id `1c72971a-b979-4459-a9c5-d006b7bff08a`).

## What's intentionally NOT in this P-item

- **Cohort backfill** of the 833 MA suspects under v4.2 prompt. Decision
  gate: if a hand-spot-check shows >50% of fingerprint hits are genuinely
  wrong (vs. legitimate SP subsections), file a follow-up P-item that runs
  `plotmax/policy/use_matrix_backfill.py` against the suspect cohort. The
  audit script's `--json` output is the cohort feed.
- **Segmenter fix**. The PDF segmenter mis-naming TABLE 1 inside a "Section
  XVII" file is the deeper cause. The locator body-pattern is a robust
  bypass — fixing the segmenter is a separate, larger workstream.
- **DB `prompt_version` enum bump**. v4.2 is doc-only; no rows need to know
  whether they were extracted under v4.1 vs v4.2 prompt text. If a future
  re-extract needs to distinguish, that re-extract creates new rows under a
  fresh `policy_extraction_run`.

## Operational runbook

### Hand-verify a single suspect

```bash
ssh plotmax-prod 'sudo -u postgres psql plotmax -c "
SELECT zd.code, ut.code, zup.status, zup.source_section,
       LEFT(zup.verbatim_quote, 60), zup.conditions_text
FROM zone_use_permission zup
JOIN zoning_districts zd ON zd.id = zup.zone_district_id
JOIN use_taxonomy ut ON ut.id = zup.use_taxonomy_id
WHERE zup.id = '\''<zup_id>'\'';
"'
```

Look at the actual bylaw section (path is recoverable via
`policy_extraction_run.crawl_artifact_id` → `crawl_artifact.storage_path`).
If the master TABLE 1 says something other than the stored `status`, file
a manual correction (clone `fix_danvers_ri_single_family.py`).

### Re-run audit after a v4.2 cohort backfill

```bash
uv run python scripts/audit_use_matrix_fingerprint.py --state MA
# Expect: count drops materially (target <100). If still >500, prompt
# v4.2 isn't doing its job; iterate on track_b_use_matrix.md.
```

### Frontend QA — verify drill-in displays verbatim

1. `pnpm dev` (web) + start FastAPI backend
2. Navigate to `/policy-review/danvers`
3. Find R-I × `residential.single_family_detached` cell
4. Click the status badge → drawer should expand showing:
   - source_section: `"Manual correction (P95)"`
   - confidence
   - (no verbatim because Danvers was hand-corrected — verbatim is NULL post-fix)

For a still-intact verbatim case, pick any other zone × use cell in any
town; the panel should expand to show `verbatim_quote` in a left-bordered
blockquote, with an amber tint + `⚠ NN chars` annotation if the verbatim
is < 50 characters long.

docs.title: Policy KG 阶段性收尾总结（v1.84.6, 2026-05-11）

versions.content:
# Policy KG 阶段性收尾总结（v1.84.6, 2026-05-11）

> 本阶段（P56 重建 → P82 use_matrix → P87 KG 前端 → P88 backfill →
> P92 audit chain）的封箱状态。下一阶段以"等人工审核 + 数据标注完成"
> 为主线，本文档冻结当前数据 + 已知问题 + 防线现状。

---

## 1. 当前数据现状（prod, MA）

### 1.1 覆盖度

| 维度 | 数字 | 覆盖率 |
|------|------|--------|
| MA total town/city | **351** | 100% (基线) |
| v4 prompt extraction runs | **301** | 86% |
| 有 `policy_rule` 数据 | **329** | 94% |
| 有 `zone_use_permission`（use matrix） | **257** | 73% |
| 缺 use matrix 的城镇 | 94 | 27% |

### 1.2 Use-matrix 质量分层（257 个有数据的城镇）

| 等级 | 标准 | 城镇数 | 占比 |
|------|------|--------|------|
| b. 1-5 uses（基本失败） | Lynnfield-class | **46** | 18% |
| c. 6-20 uses（部分） | 命中主表，漏 secondary use | **102** | 40% |
| d. 21-50 uses（良好） | 标准提取 | **95** | 37% |
| e. 51+ uses（优秀） | Hamilton / Bourne 级别 | **14** | 5% |

**结论：约 140 个城镇（46 + 94）需要 section locator 修复或重抽**。

### 1.3 Policy rule 总量

- `policy_rule` 总行数：**56,882**
- 当前生效（`is_current=true`）：**56,704**
- 已 archive（重复/旧版本）：**178**（173 来自 Essex 物理 dedup）

---

## 2. Essex County 详细状态（本阶段重点）

### 2.1 完成动作

| 动作 | 影响 |
|------|------|
| v4 backfill 32 城镇 | ~$15 LLM 成本 |
| 物理 dedup（强签名） | 173 行 archived（top: Nahant 141） |
| `audit_drill_in_dups.py` 验证 | **34/34 town × 4,300+ cell = 0 dup** ✅ |

### 2.2 Essex 34 城镇分布

| 状态 | 数量 | 城镇 |
|------|------|------|
| ✅ 良好覆盖（≥30 uses） | 6 | Boxford 35, Danvers 39, Groveland 57, Hamilton 53, Merrimac 48, Saugus 51 |
| 🟡 部分覆盖（5-30 uses） | 20 | Andover 15, Beverly 21, Newbury 20, Peabody 24, ... |
| 🔴 几乎无 use（1-4 uses） | 6 | Georgetown 7, Haverhill 4, Marblehead 2, Salem 2, Salisbury 2, Lynnfield 1 |
| ⚫ 0 uses | 8 | Amesbury, Gloucester, Ipswich, Lawrence, Lynnfield(now 1), Topsfield, Wenham |

---

## 3. 本阶段交付的 8 个 fix（v1.84.0 → v1.84.6）

| 版本 | Commit | 修的 |
|------|--------|------|
| 1.84.0 | (P92 base) | Google address search + Street View |
| 1.84.1 | `30daa5d` | use_matrix backfill dedup 改 3-tuple（不含 source_section）—— Andover 12→64 rows |
| 1.84.2 | `455ec36` | drill-in 加 use_code 过滤 applicable_use_types —— SRA × single_family 69→54 |
| 1.84.3 | `915c553` | UI: town 返回链接退到 county 页（不再退到州根） |
| 1.84.4 | `b651610` | drill-in 加 overlay_codes 过滤 + same-provision dedup —— Manchester B 40→9 |
| 1.84.5 | `2495270` | frontend `groupRulesByOverlay` undefined fix（overlay_district_id 不在 wire schema） |
| 1.84.6 | `489cbc4` | dedup signature 去掉 source_section —— Lynnfield 父+子 section 折叠 |

**Essex 物理 dedup + audit:**
- `e6cb40a` script `dedup_policy_rule_essex.py`
- `b0195cb` script `audit_drill_in_dups.py`

---

## 4. 根因分析（6 个独立 bug 叠加）

| Bug | 层级 | 表现 | 修复 |
|-----|------|------|------|
| R1 | LLM 概率模型 | 同 provision 多次抽取（windowed P88） | Write-time dedup（防线 2，未做） |
| R2 | LLM tagging 漂移 | 同行 use_categories `{*}` vs `{residential}`，section 父 vs 子 | Same as R1 |
| R3 | DB schema | 缺 UNIQUE 约束 | Alembic migration（防线 3，未做） |
| R4 | API service | 无 query-time dedup | ✅ 1.84.6 |
| R5 | Pydantic schema | `overlay_district_id` 漏出 wire | 待补 schema |
| R6 | UI grouping | strict `!== null` 把 undefined 当 overlay | ✅ 1.84.5 |

---

## 5. 4 道防线现状

```
LLM extraction → backfill 落库 → API query → UI rendering
       ↓             ↓               ↓             ↓
   [防线1]      [防线2]         [防线3]       [防线4]
   prompt        write-time      DB UNIQUE     query+UI
   收紧          dedup           constraint    dedup
   ❌            ❌              ❌            ✅
```

| 防线 | 状态 | 工作量预估 |
|------|------|------------|
| 1. LLM prompt 加 "per provision once" 指令 + few-shot | ❌ | S（修 prompt + dry-run）|
| 2. policy_rule write-time dedup | ❌ | M（加 hook 到 extractor + backfill）|
| 3. DB UNIQUE 约束（migration） | ❌ | M（必须先全 MA dedup，否则 migration fail）|
| 4. Query-time dedup + UI grouping | ✅ | done in 1.84.6 |

只有防线 4 上线就足以让**用户视角**干净。1-3 是把脏数据从 DB 清出去，并阻断未来污染。

---

## 6. 待人工审核 + 数据标注的工作（block 后续）

### 6.1 等的具体内容
- Essex 33 城镇的 policy 数据（人工 sample 校对值的真实性）
- 边缘 cell（drill-in 有显示但无人工 verify）的标注
- 高频参数（front/side/rear setback, max_height, max_far, min_lot_area）的精度审查

### 6.2 审核完成后的依据
- 标注结果作为 ground truth，反向回测 LLM extractor
- 错误模式归类（false_positive / wrong_value / wrong_unit / missing 等）
- 决定是否触发 prompt 升级（防线 1）

---

## 7. 已知问题清单（不在本阶段修）

### 7.1 数据完整性
| 问题 | 影响范围 | 修复路径 |
|------|----------|----------|
| Andover §4.1.2 主表 base zone 没抽 | Andover SRA front=35/side=20/rear=30 缺失 | LLM extractor 表格识别能力提升 |
| Lynnfield section locator 挑错 sections | 46 个 b-tier 城镇 use schedule 全空 | 改 section locator 加 use-keyword sweep |
| HMOD overlay zone FK miss | 19 行 zone=HMOD 找不到 zone_district | 给 P88 backfill 加 overlay 表 fallback resolver |
| 94 城镇完全没 use matrix | 27% 的 MA | 重跑 v4 extraction |

### 7.2 系统层
| 问题 | 影响 | 修复 |
|------|------|------|
| `RegulationRuleRead` 漏 `overlay_district_id` 字段 | 历史 wire 协议不完整 | 加字段到 schema（已用 frontend hack 绕过） |
| `policy_rule` 无 UNIQUE 约束 | 任何写入都可能制造 dup | 防线 3 migration |
| Window-based backfill 没 cross-window dedup | 同 cell 多次入库 | 防线 2 |

---

## 8. 下一阶段优先级建议（等审核完后）

### Tier A（必须）
- **A1. 等人工审核 + 标注完成** → 拿到 ground truth
- **A2. 防线 3 DB UNIQUE 约束** → 全 MA 跑 dedup → migration → 永久护栏

### Tier B（重要）
- **B1. Section locator 修复** → 解锁 46 个 b-tier + 94 个 0-use 城镇
- **B2. 防线 1 prompt 收紧** → 减少未来 LLM 重复抽取
- **B3. RegulationRuleRead schema 补 `overlay_district_id`** → 替代 frontend hack

### Tier C（可选 / 后续）
- C1. 其他 county 重复本套流程（Middlesex 54 / Worcester 56 / Norfolk 27 ...）
- C2. 防线 2 write-time dedup（防线 3 上线后这条变得可选）
- C3. Andover §4.1.2 表格能力（更大工程）

---

## 9. 待提交但已做的工作（git 上）

```
v1.84.6 origin/dev → 489cbc4 fix(policy): dedup signature
e6cb40a feat(policy): scripts/dedup_policy_rule_essex.py
fec0c5c fix(scripts): import spatial models
b0195cb feat(policy): audit_drill_in_dups.py
```

所有上线 prod。`docs/policy/stage-summary-v1.84.6.md` 本文档。

---

## 10. 关键命令快速参考

```bash
# 验证某 county 全部干净（默认 Essex）
uv run python scripts/audit_drill_in_dups.py --county Essex

# 物理 dedup（dry-run 后 --apply）
uv run python scripts/dedup_policy_rule_essex.py --county Essex --apply

# 重跑某 town v4 use_matrix backfill
uv run python scripts/backfill_use_matrix_pilot.py andover --windowed

# 看某 town 当前 use 数 + 重复率
sudo -u postgres psql plotmax -c "
  SELECT j.name, COUNT(DISTINCT ut.id) AS uses, COUNT(*) AS perms
  FROM zone_use_permission zup
  JOIN zoning_districts zd ON zd.id=zup.zone_district_id
  JOIN use_taxonomy ut ON ut.id=zup.use_taxonomy_id
  JOIN jurisdiction j ON j.id=zd.jurisdiction_id
  WHERE j.slug='<slug>' AND zup.is_current
  GROUP BY j.name;
"
```

docs.title: Use Type Pipeline (P110, v2.0.8)

versions.content:
# Use Type Pipeline (P110, v2.0.8)

> Use type 从法规文本到 `zone_use_permission` 入库的完整链路说明，包含两条并行路径、
> 已知问题清单、审计 / re-resolve 工具使用指南。

---

## 链路图

```
                      ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
                      │           zoning bylaw text                  │
                      └───────────────────┬─────────────────────────┘
                                          │
           ┌──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┐
           │ 链路 A                                                        │ 链路 B
           │ PDF/HTML master table                                        │ v4 LLM use_matrix extractor
           │ (p96_essex_cohort_runner / crawler/tasks.py)                 │ (scripts/extract_zone_use_matrix.py)
           │                                                              │
           │  extract_use_table() → ZoneUseRow[].use_label               │  LLM prompt → output canonical code
           │      ↓                                                       │      ↓
           │  UseLabelResolver(taxonomy_codes)                            │  _resolve_use_taxonomy_code(db, code)
           │    Layer 1: LABEL_ALIASES exact/substring match              │    → 精确 DB lookup (use_taxonomy.code)
           │    Layer 2: fuzzy token-overlap + SequenceMatcher            │    → 若 code 不在 DB → fallback other.other
           │    Layer 3: Gemini LLM rescue (batch 10)                     │
           │      ↓                                                       │
           │  ResolvedUse.code (or None → skip row)                       │
           └──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
                                          │
                                          ▼
                                zone_use_permission
                                (zone_district_id × use_taxonomy_id × status)
                                          │
                                          ▼
                         BuildableAreaEngine._get_permitted_uses()
                                          │
                                          ▼
                         parcel_calc_result.best_use_code
```

---

## 链路 A — PDF/HTML Master Table

**入口**：`plotmax/crawler/tasks.py:extract_master_tables` (Celery task)
         `scripts/p96_essex_cohort_runner.py` (手动批量)

**步骤**：
1. Camoufox 下载 bylaw PDF 或 HTML
2. `plotmax/policy/pdf_use_table_extractor.py:extract_use_table()` 解析 use table
3. `UseLabelResolver(taxonomy_codes)` 三层解析 use label → code
4. `apply_triples_to_db()` versioned upsert → `zone_use_permission`

**taxonomy 加载**：`_load_taxonomy()` 从 DB 动态读，每次运行加载当前全量有效 code。

**未映射行处理**：`resolve_one_fuzzy()` 返回 `code=None` 时跳过行（不写 `other.other`）。

---

## 链路 B — v4 LLM Use-Matrix Extractor

**入口**：`scripts/extract_zone_use_matrix.py` (手动 per-town)

**步骤**：
1. `section_locator.py` 定位 use-schedule 章节
2. Gemini / Claude LLM 输出结构化 `(zone_code, use_code, status)` 三元组
3. `_resolve_use_taxonomy_code(db, use_code)` 精确查找 `use_taxonomy.code`
4. **若 use_code 不在 taxonomy DB** → fallback `other.other` + 计数器 `rows_skipped_unknown_use`

**已知问题**：LLM 有时输出非标准代码（如 `residential.mixed`, `commercial.business_office`），
精确查找失败直接 fallback，原始 LLM 输出不保留。

---

## use_taxonomy 表（127 个有效 code）

| 类别 | 数量 | 示例 |
|------|------|------|
| residential | 24 | single_family_detached, two_family, townhouse, adu_* |
| commercial | 26 | retail_general, office_general, restaurant, hotel_motel |
| industrial | 15 | manufacturing_light, warehouse, distribution |
| mixed_use | 6 | general, residential_over_commercial, transit_oriented |
| institutional | 14 | school_public, hospital, religious, government |
| agricultural | 11 | farm_general, cannabis_cultivation, cannabis_dispensary |
| accessory | 13 | home_occupation, solar_panel, wind_turbine, cell_tower |
| open_space | 6 | park, conservation, wetland_natural |
| special | 7 | 40b_comprehensive_permit, mbta_communities |
| other | 1 | **other.other** (catch-all，应最小化) |

> v2.0.7 已删除 `residential.attached_dwelling`（歧义 catch-all）和
> `residential.condominium`（产权形式非用途类型）。

---

## LABEL_ALIASES 当前条目（链路 A 用）

`plotmax/policy/use_label_resolver.py:LABEL_ALIASES` 共约 85-110 条（含 P110 C2 新增），
按类别分组（节选，完整列表见源码）：

```python
# 住宅类
"single family dwelling"          → residential.single_family_detached
"sfr"                             → residential.single_family_detached
"two family dwelling"             → residential.two_family
"accessory dwelling unit"         → residential.accessory_dwelling_unit
"boarding house"                  → residential.boarding_house
"mobile home as a temporary..."   → residential.mobile_home
# 商业类
"office"                          → commercial.office_general
"retail"                          → commercial.retail_general
"restaurant sit down"             → commercial.restaurant
"motel or hotel"                  → commercial.hotel_motel
"marijuana establishments"        → agricultural.cannabis_dispensary
# 工业类
"light industrial"                → industrial.manufacturing_light
"warehouse"                       → industrial.warehouse
"contractors yard"                → industrial.contractor_yard
# 特殊
"cluster development"             → special.cluster_development
"home occupation"                 → accessory.home_occupation
```

---

## 数据质量快照（P110 审计，2026-05-28）

| 指标 | 数值 |
|------|------|
| `zone_use_permission` 总行数（is_current=TRUE）| 23,215 |
| `other.other` 行数 | 711 (3.1%) |
| 无效 taxonomy ref 行数 | 0 |
| other.other 中无 verbatim（不可重算）| ~365 |
| other.other 中有 verbatim prose（候选重算）| ~287 |

**other.other 来源分布**（估计）：
- 365 行：`verbatim_quote IS NULL`（链路 B 未填充，或旧 bootstrap 数据）
- 59 行：`[ZONE_CODE] (P96 bootstrap PDF pipeline)` — zone 代码当作 verbatim
- 287 行：实际 bylaw 文本，可尝试 re-resolve（主要包含"marijuana establishment"、"wind energy"、"solar"等）

---

## 工具使用

### 1. 数据质量审计

```bash
# 基本审计（Top 20 分布 + other.other 统计）
uv run python scripts/audit_use_taxonomy.py

# JSON 输出（供脚本消费）
uv run python scripts/audit_use_taxonomy.py --json > /tmp/audit.json

# 查看 other.other 明细（MA，前 50 行）
uv run python scripts/audit_use_taxonomy.py --detail --state MA --limit 50

# 只看无效 taxonomy ref
uv run python scripts/audit_use_taxonomy.py --invalid-only
```

### 2. 批量 re-resolve other.other 行

```bash
# dry-run（预览变更，不写入 DB）
uv run python scripts/bulk_re_resolve.py --dry-run

# 限量写入（先做小批验证）
uv run python scripts/bulk_re_resolve.py --limit 20

# 全量（MA 全州）
uv run python scripts/bulk_re_resolve.py --state MA

# 带 LLM rescue（覆盖更多行，需 Gemini API key，成本约 $0.02/10 行）
uv run python scripts/bulk_re_resolve.py --with-llm --dry-run
```

---

## 已知问题 / 后续计划

### 当前限制

1. **链路 B 不用 UseLabelResolver**：`extract_zone_use_matrix.py` 做精确 code lookup，
   LLM 输出非标准 code 直接 fallback `other.other`，无 fuzzy / LLM rescue 兜底。
   → 修复路径：在 `_resolve_use_taxonomy_code()` 失败后接入 `resolve_one_fuzzy()`（后续 P-item）

2. **other.other 的 365 行无 verbatim**：bulk_re_resolve 无法处理，需重跑原始提取。
   → `followup-backlog.md` B1 section locator 修复后可覆盖这批城镇

3. **LABEL_ALIASES 维护成本**：手工维护，MA 试点覆盖约 85-110 条，全国扩展时需系统化。
   → 长期方案：fine-tuned 专用 zoning extractor（`followup-backlog.md` Tier D3）

4. **`Marijuana Membership Club`、`Firearm Business` 等无对应 code**：
   genuinely `other.other`，后续可考虑加 taxonomy code 或保持 other.other。

### 参考文档

- `docs/policy/architecture.md` — Policy 模块总体架构
- `docs/policy/extraction-sop.md` — bylaw LLM 提取运营 SOP
- `docs/policy/followup-backlog.md` — 后续工作 Tier A/B/C 清单
- `docs/policy/p84-full-reextract-runbook.md` — 全 MA v4 重抽 runbook
- `scripts/extract_zone_use_matrix.py` — 链路 B 入口
- `plotmax/policy/use_label_resolver.py` — 链路 A resolver 实现

docs.title: cv_print Pipeline — Operator SOP (P2 v1.55.0)

versions.content:
# cv_print Pipeline — Operator SOP (P2 v1.55.0)

> Image-based zoning map → ``zoning_districts`` polygons via Gemini Vision +
> OpenCV HSV segmentation + Tesseract OCR.

---

## 1. Overview

```
artifact (image_direct, e.g. PNG/JPEG zoning map)
  │
  ├──▶ Gemini 2.5 Flash Vision  (call_vision)
  │     · GCPs (landmarks + pixel coords ± lat/lon)
  │     · Legend (district_code, district_name, color_hex)
  │
  ├──▶ Affine transform pixel → EPSG:26986
  │     · GCPs path: ≥ 3 control points with lat/lon → least-squares fit
  │     · Fallback: Jurisdiction.boundary_geom envelope → corner-to-corner
  │       affine (cv_low quality)
  │
  ├──▶ HSV color-band segmentation (OpenCV)
  │     One mask per legend.color_hex; cv2.findContours → polygons
  │
  ├──▶ Tesseract OCR per polygon bbox
  │     Cross-validate the segment-color → district_code mapping
  │
  └──▶ INSERT into zoning_districts
        source_layer='cv_print'
        quality_tier IN (cv_high, cv_medium, cv_low)
```

**Cost target**: < $0.005 / image (one Gemini Vision call). Pilot
2026-04-27 measured **$0.00065 for 2 maps** (Nahant + N. Attleborough)
→ ~$0.0003 / image, well under target.

---

## 2. System dependencies (local dev)

```bash
brew install tesseract     # macOS
# OR
apt-get install tesseract-ocr  # Linux

uv sync  # installs opencv-python-headless, pytesseract, Pillow
```

The Python deps come from `pyproject.toml`. The Tesseract binary is a
**system dep** — `pytesseract` shells out to `tesseract` on PATH. If not
installed, OCR returns empty results (pipeline still works, just degrades
to the legend-only quality path).

---

## 3. Operator SOP

### Step 0: Provision Gemini Vision API key

`/settings` page → "Google Vision API Key — cv_print" card. The key falls
back to `google_api_key` if this slot is empty (so older deployments
still work), but a **dedicated key** is recommended so Vision quota /
cost can be tracked separately from text Gemini.

### Step 1: Pick image_direct artifacts to process

```bash
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin"}' \
  | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])")

# See pending image_direct counts (post-P37)
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  http://localhost:8000/api/v1/admin/source-types/stats | jq '.zoning_map[] | select(.platform=="image_direct")'
```

### Step 2: Pilot run (5 artifacts at a time)

```bash
uv run python scripts/recrawl_cv_print_pilot.py --limit 5
```

This calls `processors/cv_print.py:process_artifact` synchronously per
artifact. Logs include:
- `p2_segment_complete` — polygons / image_size
- `p2_vectorize_complete` — cv_high/medium/low counts
- `p2_cv_print_done` — final stats + Gemini cost

### Step 3: Audit — HARD GATE 60% cv_high

```bash
uv run python scripts/audit_cv_print_quality.py
```

The audit prints per-tier and per-artifact ratios. Exits 0 only when
**cv_high ≥ 60%** of all `source_layer='cv_print'` rows. If failing:
- Check per-artifact rows — one bad image can drag the total
- Examine `raw_attributes` (ocr_text, georef_residual_m) for clues

### Step 4: Visual sign-off

Open Mapbox or QGIS, load:
- Original PNG zoning map (from `data/zoning_maps/<ds_id>/<ts>/raw/`)
- `zoning_districts` polygons for that artifact's `crawl_artifact_id`
  (PostGIS query, geom column EPSG:26986 → ST_Transform to 4326 for web)

Visually confirm polygons overlap the source map's color bands.

### Step 5: Batch process the broader pool

After pilot signs off, the same processor runs in batch mode:

```bash
uv run python -c "from plotmax.spatial.zoning_map.processors.cv_print import run; run(batch=50)"
```

Or via the existing typed-crawl admin endpoint (P36) when integrated as a
post-crawl trigger (future enhancement).

---

## 4. Quality tier rules (calibrated 2026-04-27 pilot)

| Tier | Trigger | cv_confidence |
|---|---|---:|
| **cv_high** A | GCPs with lat/lon (residual < 50 m) + OCR confirms legend code (conf > 0.7) | 0.7-0.95 |
| **cv_high** B | Vision recognized legend color → district_code AND polygon area ≥ 100 px² AND (OCR matches OR no OCR text at all) | 0.72-0.78 |
| **cv_medium** | GCP transform residual < 200 m, OR OCR partial match (conf 0.4-0.7) | 0.55-0.70 |
| **cv_low** | bbox-only fallback + no legend recognized; OR micro polygon (< 100 px²); OR OCR returned garbage | 0.3 |

**Why path B exists**: Pilot showed Gemini Vision rarely produces
reliable lat/lon for small-town zoning maps (no grid reference). The
legend-color → code mapping is itself a high-confidence signal — when
Vision hands us a clean legend AND the HSV segment finds a non-trivial
polygon, that's cv_high quality even with rough bbox-only georef.

---

## 5. Known limitations (P2 MVP scope)

- **Only `source_type='image_direct'`** — `pdf_direct` (multi-page PDF)
  and `arcgis_hub_webapp` (browser-rendered) deferred to P2.1 / P2.2
- **Bbox-fallback georef is rough** — polygons may be off by 100s of
  meters on the actual map. Operator review required before treating as
  authoritative.
- **HSV thresholds are global** (`HUE_TOL=12`, `SAT_MIN=40`, `VAL_MIN=40`)
  — pilot fine; some highly desaturated print maps may need per-state
  tuning. Future P-item.
- **Tesseract OCR** — works for printed labels ≥ 12pt; smaller fonts
  fall back to no-OCR path (still cv_high via legend recognition).
  PaddleOCR / multilingual support deferred to P2.3.
- **Single-zone maps** (e.g. Nahant has only "RES" residential) produce
  many polygons with the same code — cv_high count is inflated relative
  to information content. Future audit may add diversity weighting.

---

## 6. Failure mode quick reference

| Symptom | Cause | First check |
|---|---|---|
| `processing_status='failed'` + `image file not found on disk` | Stale `storage_path` or data dir cleanup | Check `data/zoning_maps/<storage_path>/raw/` exists |
| `processing_status='failed'` + `Vision returned empty legend` | Single-color map / Vision didn't recognize zoning context | Inspect image; consider hand-labeling a legend in artifact metadata |
| `LLMProviderError: MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL` | Gemini schema validator rejected output | Check schema doesn't use `nullable: true`; use string + `_parse_optional_float` instead |
| `Sequences of multi-polygons` | Shapely `buffer(0)` repair returned MultiPolygon | Already handled in `vectorize._project_contour`; if recurs, log polygon coordinate |
| All polygons cv_low after pilot | Vision didn't return useful legend | Inspect Vision response; check `cost_meta` to confirm call succeeded |
| Cost overrun | Vision being called multiple times per artifact | `cost_meta.estimated_cost_usd` per call is logged in `p2_cv_print_done` event |

---

## 7. Related files

- `plotmax/llm/gemini.py:call_vision` — multimodal Vision call
- `plotmax/llm/base.py:LLMProvider.call_vision` — ABC default raise
- `plotmax/spatial/zoning_map/cv/{georef,segment,ocr,vectorize}.py` — pipeline helpers
- `plotmax/spatial/zoning_map/processors/cv_print.py` — orchestrator
- `scripts/recrawl_cv_print_pilot.py` — pilot driver
- `scripts/audit_cv_print_quality.py` — HARD GATE audit
- `plotmax/settings/api.py` — `google_vision_api_key` slot

docs.title: 审核员培训手册 — Label Studio 标注流程

versions.content:
# 审核员培训手册 — Label Studio 标注流程

> 面向：第一次接触 Label Studio 的审核员
> 目标：让你能独立完成一张 zoning map 的 GCP + polygon 标注，提交后看到
> PlotMax 的 `zoning_districts` 表新增准确数据

---

## 你的工作是什么

PlotMax 后台已经爬下来很多张 zoning map（手绘 + 印刷），但电脑算法把
polygon 切歪了或者位置不准。**你的工作 = 在地图上点几个地标 + 画对几个
polygon + 提交**。每完成一张地图，PlotMax 会自动把你画的 polygon 转成
正确地理坐标存进数据库。

每张地图大概 **15-30 分钟**。

---

## 准备工作（首次需要做一次）

### 1. 浏览器开两个标签页

| Tab | 地址 | 用途 |
|---|---|---|
| Tab A | http://localhost:3000/review | PlotMax 审核队列（看任务列表 + 任务进度） |
| Tab B | http://localhost:8080 | Label Studio（实际在地图上标注） |

> Note: PlotMax 端口可能是 :3000、:59605 之类的本地 dev 端口，问 admin 要
> 准确地址；Label Studio 通常是 :8080。

### 2. Label Studio 第一次登录

打开 Tab B → 看到登录界面：

- **Email**: `admin@plotmax.local`（或 admin 给你的账号）
- **Password**: `changeme123`（或 admin 改过的）

登录成功 → 看到 **Projects** 列表。Admin 应该已经建好一个叫
"PlotMax Zoning Review"（或类似名字）的 Project。

> ❓ **看不到 Project？** 让 admin 按 `docs/spatial/label-studio.md`
> § 2c 建一个，并把你加为 member。

### 3. 准备另一个工具：Google Maps

打开 https://www.google.com/maps —— 你会反复用它查 **lat/lon 坐标**。

---

## 第一张地图：完整 walkthrough

### Step A — 在 PlotMax 选一个待审核任务

切到 **Tab A**（http://localhost:3000/review）。

页面顶部 4 个 KPI 卡片：
- 🟡 待审核 N → 你的工作池
- 🔵 审核中 N
- 🟢 已完成 N
- ⚫ 已跳过 N

下方表格按"优先级"降序排列。**优先级 100 = 普通，> 100 = 高优**。

挑一行 → 记下 4 个信息：
1. **优先级**（数字）
2. **类型**（CV 低置信 / 手绘标注）
3. **城镇**（`MA/Nahant` 这类）
4. **优先级旁边的 task id 号**（鼠标悬停行可以看）

> 💡 **建议第一张挑哪个**：选"CV 低置信" + 城镇你认识的小镇（不要选
> 大城市像 Boston 那种 polygon 几百个会累死你）。

### Step B — 在 Label Studio 找到这个任务

切到 **Tab B**。点 Projects → "PlotMax Zoning Review" → 进入项目。

左侧 task 列表，每条都有：
- Task ID（LS 自己的编号，不重要）
- **Data preview**（重要！里面应该有 `queue_id` 字段对应你 Step A 记的
  PlotMax queue id，以及 `image` 路径）

点你想标的那条 → 进入 **Labeling Interface**。

> ❓ **List 里找不到对应 queue_id？** 让 admin 把 PlotMax 队列任务
> import 进 LS。Admin 操作见 `docs/spatial/label-studio.md` § 2e。

### Step C — 标 GCP（地理控制点）

GCP = Ground Control Point = 你在地图上能识别 + 知道真实经纬度的地标。
**至少标 4 个，建议 4-6 个**。

#### 怎么选好的 GCP？

✅ **好的 GCP**（精度高）：
- 主要街道交叉口
- 市政厅 / 学校 / 教堂等明显建筑
- 桥梁两端
- 海岸线 / 湖泊岸边的拐角点
- **地图四个角落附近**各一个（这样 affine 拟合最准）

❌ **不好的 GCP**：
- 大片绿地中间（无法精确定位）
- 树林 / 田野中央
- 弯曲的河道中间（不是固定点）

#### 操作步骤

1. **看 LS 工具栏**，找到名为 `GCP` 的 KeyPoint label（红色），点它选中
2. 鼠标移到地图上你认识的第一个地标（比如某个十字路口），**点一下**
   → 出现一个红点
3. 重复 3-5 次，标 4-6 个 GCP
4. 鼠标 hover 在 GCP 上，可以拖动微调位置

#### 然后查每个 GCP 的真实坐标

切到 Google Maps：
1. 在 Google Maps 上找到对应地标（比如那个十字路口）
2. **右键** → 弹出菜单第一行就是 lat,lon（格式如 `42.4280, -70.9320`）
3. 点击就复制了

回到 LS：找到名为 `gcp_coords` 的 **TextArea 文本框**（在工具栏附近或
右侧面板里）。

按你点 GCP 的**先后顺序**，一行写一个 lat,lon：

```
42.4280,-70.9320
42.4280,-70.9100
42.4180,-70.9100
42.4180,-70.9320
```

> ⚠️ **顺序极其重要**！第 1 行 lat/lon 必须对应你点的第 1 个 GCP，第 2
> 行对应第 2 个，以此类推。如果记不住顺序，只标 4 个角的 GCP（NW NE SE
> SW 顺时针），相对好记。

### Step D — 画 zoning polygon

#### 操作步骤

1. 看 LS 工具栏的 **PolygonLabels** 区域，里面有 R-1 / R-2 / C-1 / I-1 /
   OS 等 button（admin 配置的）。
2. 找到对应原图上某个 zoning 区域 —— 比如黄色块代表"R-1 单户住宅"
3. 点 LS 工具栏的 **R-1** label 选中
4. 在地图上 **沿着黄色块的边缘点击顶点**（每点一下加一个顶点）
5. 闭合 polygon：双击最后一个点 OR 回到第一个点点击
6. 完成后这个 polygon 会以选中颜色显示出来

#### 一张地图通常有几个 polygon？

- 小镇（如 Nahant）：1-5 个
- 中等城市：10-30 个
- 大城市（Boston / Cambridge）：50+

新手第一张 **建议从 polygon 数 < 10 的小镇开始**。

#### 不同 zoning code 的颜色对照

按原始 zoning map 的图例（legend）来对应：
- **黄色 / 浅黄** → 通常是 R-1 (单户住宅)
- **橙色 / 浅橙** → R-2 R-3 (多户)
- **红色** → C-1 商业
- **紫色** → I-1 工业
- **绿色** → OS Open Space 公共绿地
- **混色** → MU 混合用途

不确定就**让 admin 帮看一下原图 legend**。

### Step E — Submit

确认 4-6 个 GCP 都标了 + lat/lon 都填了 + 至少 1 个 polygon 画完了。

点 **Submit** 按钮（通常在右下角）。

LS 会发送 webhook 到 PlotMax 后端。**不需要你额外做任何事**。

### Step F — 验证标注成功

切到 **Tab A**（PlotMax `/review`），按 F5 刷新。

期望看到：
1. **顶部 KPI**："已完成"数字 +1，"待审核"数字 -1
2. **表格筛选选 "已完成"** → 你刚标的城镇出现在列表里，状态绿色"已完成"
3. **备注字段**应该没有错误（如果有错误，会写"(p3) only N GCPs..."之类）

✅ 看到上面三条就 **完成一张地图**！

---

## 常见错误 + 修复

### 错误 1: 提交后状态变成"已跳过"，备注 "only 2 GCPs provided"

**原因**：你只点了少于 3 个 GCP。
**修复**：回 LS 同一个 task → 加更多 GCP → 重新 Submit。

### 错误 2: "GCPs lack lat/lon — fill gcp_coords textarea"

**原因**：你点了 GCP 但忘记在文本框里填 lat/lon。
**修复**：回 LS → 找 `gcp_coords` 文本框 → 按 GCP 顺序填 lat/lon → Submit。

### 错误 3: "georef residual 850m > 200m threshold"

**原因**：你的某个 GCP 的 lat/lon 写错了（比如把两个 GCP 的坐标颠倒，或
某个 lat 多打了一位数）。
**修复**：
1. 仔细对照 Google Maps 检查每个 GCP 的 lat/lon 是不是和你点的位置匹配
2. 检查 lat/lon 的**顺序**（lat 在前，lon 在后；MA 大概是 lat=42 lon=-71）
3. 改完重新 Submit

> 💡 提示：MA 的合理坐标范围是 lat ∈ [41.2, 42.9], lon ∈ [-73.5, -69.9]。
> 超出这个范围基本是写错了。

### 错误 4: "image not on disk; re-crawl source"

**原因**：原图文件被清理了（不是你的错）。
**修复**：跟 admin 说，需要 `force_recrawl` 那个 source（运维操作）。

### 错误 5: LS 里 polygon 边界画歪了 / 漏了一块

**修复**：在 LS 里 polygon 还没 Submit 前，可以拖动顶点 / 加新顶点 / 删
顶点（hover 一个 polygon → 看右键菜单）。Submit 后想改？再次提交一个
"ANNOTATION_UPDATED"（LS 自动）就会覆盖前一版（PlotMax 的 idempotent
delete-then-insert 兜住）。

---

## 高效工作流（练熟之后）

每完成一张地图大概 **15-30 分钟**，这是普通节奏：

- 5 分钟：研究原图 legend + 决定标哪些 polygon
- 5 分钟：点 4 个角的 GCP + 查 lat/lon
- 10-15 分钟：画 polygons
- 1 分钟：Submit + 验证

加快节奏小技巧：

1. **GCP 只标 4 个角**（NW NE SE SW），最简单且 affine 拟合精度最高
2. **先点完所有 GCP，再统一切到 Google Maps 查 4 个 lat/lon**（避免来回切）
3. **复制粘贴 lat/lon**：Google Maps 右键第一行直接是 `42.42, -70.93`
   格式，复制回 LS 文本框就行（注意改成 `42.42,-70.93` 一行一个）
4. **小 polygon 用 4 个点就够**，不用沿着曲折边界精细描

---

## 进度看板

随时回 Tab A `/review` 页面看进度：

- **顶部 KPI**：今天完成多少 / 队列还剩多少
- **筛选 "已完成"**：你之前标过的所有任务
- **筛选 "已跳过"** + 看备注：之前失败的，可以打开重新标

---

## 何时找 admin

- 第一次登录登不上 → admin 重置密码
- LS 里看不到 task → admin 帮 import
- task 备注里出现 "image not on disk" → admin 重新爬
- 不确定某个 zoning code 颜色对应 → admin 看原图 legend
- 标了 10+ 个连续 task 都报 residual 太高 → admin 检查 LS 配置 / webhook

---

## 完整链路示意

```
你（审核员）
   │
   ├─ Tab A /review 选 task
   ├─ Tab B Label Studio 标 GCP + 画 polygon + 填 lat/lon
   └─ 点 Submit
                │
                ▼
      Label Studio 发 webhook (HMAC 签名)
                │
                ▼
      PlotMax 后端 /api/v1/zoning-review/webhook
        · 校验签名
        · 用 4 个 GCP 算 affine transform（pixel → 真实经纬度）
        · polygon pixel 坐标 → EPSG:26986 真实地理坐标
        · INSERT zoning_districts (source_layer='manual_label')
        · 队列状态翻 "已完成"
        · 原图 + 标注 JSON 归档到 data/training/zoning_maps/handdrawn/
                │
                ▼
      PlotMax `/review` 页 30 秒后自动刷新 → 你看到任务变绿
```

---

## 快速参考

| 我要... | 做这个 |
|---|---|
| 看待审核任务列表 | Tab A `/review` |
| 进入标注界面 | Tab B Label Studio → Project → 选 task → Label |
| 查地点 lat/lon | Google Maps 右键 → 第一行 |
| 知道哪个颜色对应哪个 zoning code | 看原图右边/角落的 **Legend** |
| 提交标注 | LS 里点 Submit 按钮 |
| 看自己标过哪些 | Tab A `/review` 筛选 "已完成" |
| 看失败原因 | Tab A `/review` 筛选 "已跳过" 看备注 |
| 改之前提交的 | LS 里同一 task 重新打开 + 修改 + Submit |

---

**第一张如果卡住别硬扛**，截图 + 备注问 admin。第二张开始就熟了。

docs.title: Label Studio Human Review — Operator SOP (P3 v1.56.0 → P53 v1.57.0)

versions.content:
# Label Studio Human Review — Operator SOP (P3 v1.56.0 → P53 v1.57.0)

> Take cv_print pipeline's low-confidence + handdrawn + **PDF (P53)** zoning
> maps, hand them to a human for GCP marking + polygon drawing in Label
> Studio (LS), and webhook the annotations back into
> ``zoning_districts(source_layer='manual_label')`` with proper EPSG:26986
> georeferencing.

---

## 1. Architecture

```
PDF zoning maps (4806 sources, ~126 with crawl_artifact on disk)
   │
   ▼  scripts/convert_pdf_zoning_maps.py  (P53 — PyMuPDF render → raw/zoning_map.png)
   │
zoning_map_review_queue (cv_print + handdrawn + annotate_pdf_map)
   │
   ▼  scripts/import_to_label_studio.py  (P53 — session-cookie auth + batch import)
   │
Label Studio @ :8080  ← tasks reference /plotmax-data/<storage_path>/raw/zoning_map.png
   │
   │  Operator places 4-6 GCPs + types lat/lon for each + draws polygons
   │
   ▼  POST webhook (HMAC SHA256 signed)
PlotMax /api/v1/zoning-review/webhook
   │  · verify HMAC against label_studio_webhook_secret
   │  · parse keypoints (% pixel) + textarea lat/lon (CSV per line)
   │  · call georef.from_gcps (P2 reuse) → affine transform pixel → EPSG:26986
   │  · project polygon contours through transform → MultiPolygon
   │  · INSERT zoning_districts(source_layer='manual_label', quality_tier='manual')
   │  · queue.status = 'done', completed_at = now
   │  · archive raw image + annotation.json to data/training/zoning_maps/handdrawn/
   ▼
Mapbox / dashboard now shows authoritative manual_label districts
```

---

## 2. One-time setup

### 2a. Start Label Studio

```bash
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d label-studio

# Wait for health
until curl -fs http://localhost:8080/health >/dev/null; do sleep 4; done

# Login at http://localhost:8080
#   Username: admin@plotmax.local
#   Password: changeme123  (or LABEL_STUDIO_PASSWORD env)
```

### 2b. Generate + set webhook secret

```bash
SECRET=$(python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(32))")
echo "Save this: $SECRET"

# Either via UI: /settings → "Label Studio Webhook Secret" card → paste secret
# OR via API:
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin"}' \
  | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])")
curl -s -X PUT -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d "{\"label_studio_webhook_secret\":\"$SECRET\"}" \
  http://localhost:8000/api/v1/settings/llm
```

### 2c. Create LS Project + label config

In Label Studio UI → **Create Project** → "Labeling Setup" → **Custom template**.
Paste this XML:

```xml
<View>
  <Image name="image" value="$image" zoom="true"/>

  <KeyPoint name="gcps" toName="image" maxPoints="6">
    <Label value="GCP" background="red"/>
  </KeyPoint>

  <TextArea name="gcp_coords" toName="gcps"
            placeholder="42.345,-71.062 (one line per GCP, in same order)"
            rows="6" required="true"/>

  <PolygonLabels name="zones" toName="image">
    <Label value="R-1" background="yellow"/>
    <Label value="R-2" background="orange"/>
    <Label value="R-3" background="lightgreen"/>
    <Label value="C-1" background="cyan"/>
    <Label value="I-1" background="purple"/>
    <Label value="OS"  background="green"/>
    <!-- Add per-town district codes from the legend -->
  </PolygonLabels>
</View>
```

### 2d. Configure webhook in LS Project

LS Project Settings → **Webhooks** → **Add Webhook**:
- URL: `http://host.docker.internal:8000/api/v1/zoning-review/webhook`
- Method: POST
- Secret: paste the same $SECRET from 2b
- Send for: ✅ Annotation created, ✅ Annotation updated

LS sends header `X-Hook-Signature` (some versions) or `X-LabelStudio-Signature`
— PlotMax accepts the latter; if your LS sends the former, route via reverse
proxy or update header alias in `plotmax/spatial/review/api.py`.

### 2e. Import tasks

**Recommended path (P53 v1.57.0): batch script.**

```bash
# 0. seed LS project id (one-time per env)
uv run python scripts/seed_ls_project_id.py 4

# 1. PDF sources → PNG (idempotent — skips already-converted artifacts)
uv run python scripts/convert_pdf_zoning_maps.py
# Use --jurisdiction <slug> + --dry-run to preview first; --split-pages
# when a multi-page PDF needs each page as its own LS task.

# 2. enqueue review_queue rows for newly-PNG'd PDF artifacts
uv run python plotmax/spatial/zoning_map/processors/manual.py --batch 1000

# 3. push pending tasks to LS Project N
uv run python scripts/import_to_label_studio.py --task-type annotate_pdf_map
# Skips rows whose raw/zoning_map.png is missing on disk (broken-image guard).
# Marks each pushed row's label_studio_task_id so re-runs are idempotent.
```

PDF rendering notes:
- **Vector PDFs** (typical municipal zoning bylaws): text + lines + fills
  stay sharp. Default 144 dpi (PyMuPDF Matrix(2,2)) is enough for GCP
  placement.
- **Scan-only PDFs** (older towns): rasterize as-is — no OCR. Operator
  decides per-task whether the scan is legible enough; skip via /review
  UI if not.
- **Multi-page PDFs**: default vertical-merge into one tall ``zoning_map.png``
  (max 20 pages safety cap). Use ``--split-pages`` for per-page tasks.

**MVP path (manual)** — for each queue task you want to review:

```bash
# Get a task from the queue
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  'http://localhost:8000/api/v1/zoning-review/queue?status=pending&limit=1' | jq '.items[0]'

# Note the storage_path; the LS-mounted path is /plotmax-data/<storage_path>/raw/<filename>
# Build LS task JSON:
cat > /tmp/ls_task.json <<JSON
[
  {
    "data": {
      "image": "/plotmax-data/zoning_maps/<ds_id>/<ts>/raw/zoning_map.png",
      "queue_id": <queue_id>
    }
  }
]
JSON

# Import via LS UI (Project → Import → drop the JSON file)
# OR via LS API (replace <PROJECT_ID> + <LS_API_TOKEN>):
curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Token <LS_API_TOKEN>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @/tmp/ls_task.json \
  http://localhost:8080/api/projects/<PROJECT_ID>/import
```

The crucial bit is `task.data.queue_id` — webhook handler uses it to resolve
back to the right `zoning_map_review_queue` row.

---

## 3. Operator workflow

For each task:

1. **Open task in LS** (will show the zoning map image)
2. **Place 4-6 KeyPoints** on recognizable landmarks. Best targets:
   - Major street intersections
   - Town hall / municipal buildings (clearly labeled)
   - Lake centroids / beach corners
   - Major bridge endpoints
   Avoid: large green parks (low geo-precision), tree canopies
3. **Type lat/lon** in the `gcp_coords` TextArea, one per line:
   ```
   42.4280,-70.9320
   42.4280,-70.9100
   42.4180,-70.9100
   42.4180,-70.9320
   ```
   Get coords from Google Maps: right-click any landmark → first menu item is
   the lat/lon, click to copy.
4. **Draw polygons** for each zoning district. Click district label first,
   then click vertices (close polygon by double-clicking the first point).
5. **Submit** — LS sends webhook to PlotMax, polygon → zoning_districts
   immediately.

The `/review` page (PlotMax UI :3000/review) auto-refreshes every 30s; the
task should flip from `pending` → `done` within seconds of submission.

---

## 4. Quality gates

Webhook applies these guards (artifact stays `pending` if any fails — operator
sees `notes` updated, can re-submit):

| Guard | Threshold | Failure note |
|---|---|---|
| Min GCPs | ≥ 3 | "(p3) only N GCPs provided; need >= 3" |
| GCPs have lat/lon | All ≥ 3 must parse | "(p3) GCPs lack lat/lon — fill gcp_coords textarea" |
| Affine residual | < 200 m | "(p3) georef residual Xm > 200m threshold; re-place GCPs" |
| Image on disk | exists | "(p3) raw image missing on disk; re-crawl source" |

`zoning_districts` rows from this pipeline get
`source_layer='manual_label'`, `quality_tier='manual'`,
`cv_confidence=1.00`, `needs_review=False` — they are authoritative,
overriding any earlier `cv_print` rows for the same artifact via the
delete-then-insert idempotency in `vectorize.insert_districts`.

Wait — actually `vectorize.insert_districts` only deletes prior `cv_print`
rows, not `manual_label`. The webhook handler appends manual rows; cv_print
rows from prior runs may coexist. Consumer (frontend / Mapbox) should prefer
`manual_label` over `cv_print` when both exist for the same district code.

---

## 5. End-to-end smoke test

Verified 2026-04-27 with synthetic payload on Nahant (queue 257):

```bash
PAYLOAD='<ls webhook json with 4 GCPs + 1 polygon>'
SIG=$(echo -n "$PAYLOAD" | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac "$SECRET" -hex | awk '{print $2}')
curl -X POST -H "X-LabelStudio-Signature: sha256=$SIG" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$PAYLOAD" \
  http://localhost:8000/api/v1/zoning-review/webhook
```

Response:
```json
{
  "queue_id": 257,
  "artifact_id": "1df730e2-...",
  "gcps_used": 4,
  "polygons_inserted": 1,
  "georef_residual_m": 0.07,
  "status_after": "done",
  "error": null
}
```

DB check:
```sql
SELECT code, source_layer, quality_tier
FROM zoning_districts
WHERE source_layer='manual_label'
ORDER BY imported_at DESC LIMIT 1;
-- code | source_layer  | quality_tier
-- R-1  | manual_label  | manual
```

---

## 6. Training dataset archive

Every successful webhook copies:
- The raw image (e.g. `zoning_map.png`)
- The full LS annotation JSON

To `data/training/zoning_maps/handdrawn/{queue_id}/`. Used by future ML
training (active learning loop, fine-tuning a custom polygon detector,
etc.). MVP stores **all** review types here — the `task_type` field in
`annotation.json` distinguishes `review_cv_low` vs `annotate_handdrawn`.

---

## 7. Failure mode quick reference

| Symptom | Cause | First check |
|---|---|---|
| 401 invalid HMAC signature | LS Webhook secret mismatch | Re-paste secret in LS Project + `/settings` |
| 503 not configured | `label_studio_webhook_secret` empty | Set in `/settings` page |
| `status_after='ignored'` | LS sent non-annotation event | Filter LS webhook to only ANNOTATION_CREATED/UPDATED |
| `status_after='skipped'` + "too few GCPs" | Operator submitted with < 3 keypoints | Re-open task in LS, add more GCPs |
| `status_after='skipped'` + "GCPs lack lat/lon" | Operator forgot to fill `gcp_coords` TextArea | Fill it, re-submit |
| `status_after='skipped'` + "residual too high" | GCP lat/lon typo OR landmark too imprecise | Verify each GCP's coords on Google Maps; replace large-area landmarks with intersections |
| `status_after='skipped'` + "image not on disk" | Storage path stale (data dir cleanup) | Re-crawl the source via P36 force_recrawl |

---

## 8. Related files

- `docker-compose.dev.yml` — label-studio service
- `plotmax/settings/api.py` — `label_studio_webhook_secret` + (P53) `label_studio_project_id` slots
- `plotmax/spatial/review/{schemas,service,api}.py` — backend (dual-auth webhook: HMAC OR Bearer)
- `plotmax/spatial/zoning_map/processors/manual.py` — queue enqueue (4 OR criteria including PDF)
- `plotmax/spatial/zoning_map/processors/pdf_to_png.py` — **P53** PDF rasterizer
- `scripts/convert_pdf_zoning_maps.py` — **P53** batch PDF → PNG CLI
- `scripts/import_to_label_studio.py` — **P53** LS Project auto-import
- `scripts/seed_ls_project_id.py` — **P53** bootstrap helper
- `web/app/review/page.tsx` — operator queue UI (filter dropdown supports `annotate_pdf_map`)
- `tests/unit/spatial/test_p3_review_api.py` — 16 contract tests
- `tests/unit/spatial/test_p53_pdf_to_png.py` — **P53** 8 converter tests
- `tests/unit/spatial/test_p53_enqueue_pdf.py` — **P53** 7 contract tests

docs.title: Mapviewer × Google Maps Platform 集成

versions.content:
# Mapviewer × Google Maps Platform 集成

> 范围：`/spatial/mapviewer` 页面的 **地址搜索** 与 **街景** 两个交互
> 复用 Google Maps Platform；Mapbox 继续负责底图渲染 + WMS 叠加层。
> 本文档说明 Google API 的选用、计费、GCP 配置 SOP、运行时兜底，以及未来扩展。

---

## 为什么不用 Mapbox 自带的搜索

| 维度 | Mapbox Geocoding | Google Places API (New) |
|---|---|---|
| 美国住宅地址精度 | 较好 | **更好** —— 商住地址、苹果地址、新建小区都覆盖 |
| 邮编检索 + 区域 viewport | 仅返回中心点 | 返回 viewport 矩形，直接 `fitBounds` |
| 街景 | **无** | StreetViewPanorama + Pegman 标准方案 |
| 月免费额度 | 100K req | $300 credit ≈ 100K Autocomplete + 40K JS map load |
| Token 安全 | 浏览器 referer 限制 | 浏览器 referer 限制（同模式） |

Mapbox 的 token + 渲染管线被 P89/P91 已经验证稳定；本 P-item **不动 Mapbox**，只在搜索 / 街景两条线接 Google。

---

## 接入的 4 个 API

| API | 用途 | Endpoint | 计价 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Places API (New) — Autocomplete** | 输入框下拉补全 | `POST /v1/places:autocomplete` | $2.83 / 1K **session**（一组 keystroke + 1 次 details = 1 session） |
| **Places API (New) — Place Details** | 选中后取 lat/lng + viewport | `GET /v1/places/{placeId}` | $17 / 1K（fields-mask 锁 Pro tier） |
| **Maps JavaScript API** | 加载 SDK + StreetViewPanorama | `<script src="maps/api/js?...">` | $7 / 1K JS map load |
| **Street View Static API** ⭕ 可选 | 候选缩略图（本 P-item 未启用） | `/maps/api/streetview` | $7 / 1K |

> ⚠️ **不启用 Geocoding API**（已实测）—— 该 API 不接受 referer-restricted key，前端用不了。Places API New 的 Autocomplete + Details 已覆盖正向地址→坐标。

### Field-mask 必须锁定

`Place Details` 的计费等级由 `X-Goog-FieldMask` 决定：

```
location,formattedAddress,displayName,types,viewport
```

任何 PR 想加 `reviews` / `photos` 都会跳到 **Enterprise tier $55/1K**，单价翻 3 倍。`google-places.test.mjs` 里有显式断言：

```javascript
test("DETAILS_FIELDS — locked-down field mask (no costly fields)", () => {
  assert.equal(
    _internal.DETAILS_FIELDS,
    "location,formattedAddress,displayName,types,viewport",
  );
});
```

CR 看到改 mask 必须警觉。

### Session token 必须复用

Autocomplete 计价规则：**只要同一个 sessionToken 串起 N 次 Autocomplete + 最多 1 次 Place Details，整组算一个 session**。`address-search.tsx` 里：

```typescript
const [sessionToken, setSessionToken] = useState(() => genSessionToken());
// ...
const handleSelect = async (p) => {
  const place = await placeDetails(p.placeId, sessionToken);  // 同一个 token
  // ...
  setSessionToken(genSessionToken());  // 选中后立刻重置，下一轮 session
};
```

如果漏复用 → 计价从 $2.83/1K session 跳到 $17/1K per-request，单价直接 **6×**。

---

## 一次性 GCP 配置 SOP

### Step 1 — 项目准备

1. https://console.cloud.google.com → 新建或选定项目
2. **Billing 必须绑定结算账户**（Maps Platform 强制要求；$300 free credit/月覆盖团队内部使用）
3. 设 **Billing → Budgets & alerts** 月度预算（建议 $50-100），50%/90%/100% 邮件告警
4. （可选）**Pub/Sub → Cloud Function** 触顶时自动 disable billing

### Step 2 — 启用 API

**APIs & Services → Library** 搜索并启用：
- ✅ **Places API (New)**（不是旧版 `Places API`）
- ✅ **Maps JavaScript API**
- ⭕ **Street View Static API**（如未来加候选缩略图）

### Step 3 — 创建 API key + 限制

**APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → API key**

立刻点 **Edit API key** 配置：

**Application restrictions** → **HTTP referrers (websites)**：
```
https://ai.plot-max.com/*
https://ai.plot-max.com
http://localhost:3000/*
```

> 通配符 `*://*.plot-max.com/*` GCP 不支持，逐行写。

**API restrictions** → **Restrict key** → 勾选启用的所有 API。

### Step 4 — Quota cap（防超支）

**APIs & Services → Quotas** 按 API 设单日上限：

| API | Quota 项 | 推荐 |
|---|---|---|
| Places API (New) | Autocomplete requests / day | 5,000 |
| Places API (New) | Place Details requests / day | 1,000 |
| Maps JavaScript API | Map loads / day | 5,000 |

### Step 5 — 把 key 写到环境变量

**Prod**（deploy-script-safe 路径）：
```bash
ssh plotmax-prod
cd /opt/plotmax/plotmax2.0
echo 'NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY=AIzaSy...' >> web/.env.production.local
sudo systemctl restart plotmax-nextjs
```

**Dev**：
```bash
echo 'NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY=AIzaSy...' >> /Users/hsueh/PlotMaxV2.0/web/.env.local
```

### Step 6 — 自检

```bash
KEY="AIzaSy..."
# 应返回 403（无 referer）
curl -s -X POST "https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-Goog-Api-Key: $KEY" \
  -H "X-Goog-FieldMask: suggestions.placePrediction.placeId" \
  -d '{"input":"Newton"}'
# 应返回 200
curl -s -X POST "https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Referer: https://ai.plot-max.com/x" \
  -H "X-Goog-Api-Key: $KEY" \
  -H "X-Goog-FieldMask: suggestions.placePrediction.placeId" \
  -d '{"input":"Newton"}'
```

---

## 前端架构

```
[address-search.tsx]
  │  debounce 300ms
  ├──► google-places.placesAutocomplete(input, sessionToken)
  │      └─ POST /v1/places:autocomplete
  │         body: { input, locationBias: rect MA, includedRegionCodes: [us], sessionToken }
  │         返回 PlacePrediction[] { placeId, mainText, secondaryText, types }
  │
  ├──► dropdown 渲染候选 (mainText + secondaryText + 类型徽章)
  │
  └──► 用户点选 → google-places.placeDetails(placeId, sessionToken)
        └─ GET /v1/places/{placeId}?sessionToken=...
           headers: X-Goog-FieldMask: location,formattedAddress,displayName,types,viewport
           返回 PlaceLocation { lat, lng, formattedAddress, displayName, types, viewport? }
              ↓
[mapviewer/page.tsx] handleAddressSelect()
  │  if (place.viewport) → MapViewer.fitBounds(viewport)        // zip / locality / county
  │  else                → MapViewer.flyTo(zoom = pickZoomForTypes(types))  // street address
  │  if (isPointResult(types)) → setMarker({ lng, lat, label })
  │
  └──► 用户点 marker 或 "街景" 按钮
          ↓
[street-view-panel.tsx]
```
