```
  ├──► loadMapsSDK(key)  // lazy: 首次开街景才下载 681KB JS bundle
  │      └─ <script async callback=__plotmax_gm_init>
  │
  └──► google.maps.StreetViewService.getPanorama({ location, radius: 50 })
        ├─ status OK    → new google.maps.StreetViewPanorama(...)
        └─ ZERO_RESULTS → "该位置 50 米内无街景" fallback
```

---

## 运行时兜底

| 失败场景 | 行为 |
|---|---|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY` 未配置 | Autocomplete 返回 `[]` + console.warn；街景面板显示"未配置 key" |
| Autocomplete HTTP 4xx/5xx | 返回 `[]` + console.warn；下拉空 |
| Place Details 失败 | 不调 `onSelect`，搜索框保持原状 |
| Maps JS SDK 加载失败 | 街景面板显示"街景加载失败" |
| `getPanorama` ZERO_RESULTS | 街景面板显示"50 米内无街景" |
| referer 配置错（401） | 同上，浏览器 console 看到 `API_KEY_HTTP_REFERRER_BLOCKED` |
| Billing cap 触发 | 同上各种 401/403；UI 不崩溃 |

**核心原则：UI 永不崩溃**。Google 任何 API 调用失败都不应让页面白屏，搜索 / 底图 / WMS 仍然能用。

---

## 类型粒度 → 缩放映射

`pickZoomForTypes(types)` 决定 flyTo 的 zoom 值：

| Place type | 含义 | Mapbox zoom |
|---|---|---|
| `street_address` / `premise` / `subpremise` | 具体地址 / 楼宇 | **17**（地块级） |
| `postal_code` | 邮编 | **13**（街区级） |
| `locality` / `administrative_area_level_3` | 城镇 | **12**（城镇级） |
| `administrative_area_level_2` | 县 | **10**（县级） |
| 其他 | fallback | **14** |

**优先级：viewport > 类型映射**。如果 Place Details 返回 `viewport`（zip/locality/county 通常会），用 `fitBounds` 自动缩到合适范围；只有点位结果（地址）走 zoom-based flyTo。

---

## 未来扩展（不在本 P-item 内）

- **反向 geocoding**（点地图 → 地址）：需要 Geocoding API，但该 API 不接受 referer-restricted key —— 走后端 proxy 转发或单独申请 IP-restricted key
- **候选缩略图**：每条 dropdown 候选右侧显示 Static Street View 100×60px 缩略图 → +5 张 / 搜索 ≈ $0.035/搜索；体验提升明显
- **历史搜索 + 收藏**：localStorage 存最近 5 条 + 用户主动 ★ 收藏的常用地址
- **Place Autocomplete 缓存**：相同 query 串 5 分钟内复用结果，省 session
- **国际化**：当前 `locationBias` 锁 MA + `includedRegionCodes: us`；扩到全美/全球时调这两个字段

docs.title: NZA Mapviewer Layer (P94)

versions.content:
# NZA Mapviewer Layer (P94)

> Renders the local mirror of National Zoning Atlas (NZA) slice data as
> a Mapbox vector layer in `/spatial/mapviewer`, colour-coded by
> single-family treatment with a click popup showing all family-tier
> details. Sits alongside the existing MassGIS WMS catalog.

---

## Architecture

```
┌────────────────────┐
│  Mapviewer page    │
│  ┌─────────────┐   │
│  │ NZA toggle  │ ─►─┐
│  └─────────────┘   │
│                    │
│  ┌─────────────────▼──────────────────────────────┐
│  │  map-viewer.tsx                                │
│  │  - on moveend (zoom>=10 & showNza):            │
│  │      api.getNzaSlicesGeoJSON({bbox})           │
│  │  - source.setData(featureCollection)           │
│  │  - layer-scoped click → onNzaFeatureClick      │
│  └────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘
│           │
│           ▼
│      ┌─────────────────────┐
│      │  Mapbox GL          │
│      │  GeoJSON source     │
│      │  Fill + Line layers │
│      │  minzoom=10         │
│      └─────────────────────┘
│
│  Backend: GET /api/v1/spatial/nza/slices.geojson
│           ?bbox=min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat
│           &state=MA (optional)
│           &limit=≤5000 (server cap)
│
│  Service: plotmax/spatial/nza/service.py
│           ST_AsGeoJSON + ST_Intersects(ST_MakeEnvelope)
│           Decimal → float coercion for Mapbox JSON
└────────────────────┘
```

---

## Performance design

| Concern | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| MA z=8 viewport = 11K slices = 30MB JSON | **Hard cap 5000 features server-side**; UI tells user above z<10 the slice density is illegible anyway |
| Viewport pan triggers refetch | Throttled by Mapbox `moveend` (~debounced by Mapbox internals); fetch-token cancellation ensures last-fetch wins |
| GeoJSON decode on main thread | Mapbox's GeoJSON worker handles parsing off main thread |
| setStyle wipes sources | The sync effect re-runs on `styleLoaded`, so basemap switches automatically re-attach the NZA layer |

Empirical: MA z=10 viewport → ~500-1000 slices → ~2MB JSON → ~150ms fetch + ~80ms Mapbox decode. Well below user-perceptible threshold.

---

## Color scheme (family1_treatment)

| Treatment | Color | Tailwind | Hex |
|---|---|---|---|
| allowed | 🟢 | green-500 | `#22c55e` |
| special_permit | 🟡 | yellow-500 | `#eab308` |
| prohibited | 🔴 | red-500 | `#ef4444` |
| overlay | 🔵 | blue-500 | `#3b82f6` |
| (null/unknown) | ⚫ | neutral-400 | `#a3a3a3` |

Fill opacity 0.45; outline grey-800 at 0.6 opacity. Chosen to read well over both light-v10 and streets-v11 basemaps.

family2/3/4 tiers are not surfaced in the fill color (would be cognitive overload) — they appear in the click popup.

---

## Click popup

`NzaSlicePopup` (absolute-positioned card top-right) shows:

- Zone name + jurisdiction + state + district_type
- 4 family tiers, each with color swatch + treatment label
- Family1 min lot acres (handles single value + min/max range triplet)
- Family1 height cap (boolean flag)
- ADU treatment + owner-occupancy requirement
- Slice id (footer, for cross-ref with audit JSON)

---

## API reference

```
GET /api/v1/spatial/nza/slices.geojson
    ?bbox=<min_lon>,<min_lat>,<max_lon>,<max_lat>   (required, EPSG:4326)
    &state=<2-letter>                                (optional)
    &limit=<int, 1-5000>                             (optional, default 5000)

Response:
{
  "type": "FeatureCollection",
  "metadata": {
    "bbox": [-72.0, 42.0, -71.0, 43.0],
    "state_filter": "MA",
    "feature_count": 1234,
    "limit_applied": 5000,
    "truncated": false
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "type": "Feature",
      "geometry": { "type": "MultiPolygon", "coordinates": [...] },
      "properties": {
        "nza_slice_id": 982711,
        "name": "G + WTOD",
        "district_type": "R",
        "family1_treatment": "allowed",
        "family1_minlotacres": 1.0,
        "family1_heightcap": true,
        ... (40+ fields)
        "jurisdiction_name": "Hancock",
        "jurisdiction_state": "MA"
      }
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

Authentication: requires a valid PlotMax JWT (admin / editor / viewer all OK — read-only data).

---

## URL state

The mapviewer page persists NZA toggle into the URL: `?nza=1`. Combined with `?layers=...` and `?basemap=...` from earlier P-items, the entire view is fully shareable.

---

## Coverage today

| State | NZA slices in DB | mapviewer-ready |
|---|---|---|
| MA | 11,395 | ✅ |
| NY | 11,892 | ✅ |
| GA | 4,980 | ✅ |
| CT | 4,299 | ✅ |
| VT | 3,631 | ✅ |
| ME | 3,573 | ✅ |
| MI | 2,630 | ✅ |
| WA | 2,629 | ✅ |
| NH | 1,752 | ✅ |
| RI | 803 | ✅ |
| MT | 685 | ✅ |
| Total | **49,606** | |

(Plus 1,623 spillover slices from non-imported states via NZA's CONUS-wide jurisdictions layer.)

---

## Known limitations / future work

| Limitation | Mitigation strategy |
|---|---|
| `slice.name = "G + WTOD"` base+overlay names don't auto-split | Future P-item: parse `"X + Y"` → render two semi-transparent fills stacked |
| MapBox struggles >5K features in one viewport | Lower the limit if we move to vector tile server (martin) for true global coverage |
| family1-only color encoding | Future toggle: select which family tier drives the fill |
| No raster export | Mapbox `getCanvas().toDataURL()` if a "export this view as PNG" need arises |

docs.title: NZA Tile Sync (P78)

versions.content:
# NZA Tile Sync (P78)

**Module**: `plotmax/spatial/nza/`
**Tables**: `nza_jurisdiction`, `nza_zoning_district`, `nza_zoning_slice`
**Migrations**: `w001_p78_nza_tables`, `w002_p78_nza_slice_field_types`
**APScheduler job ID**: `nza_sync`
**Default cron**: every Monday 03:00 ET (off by default; admin enables)

---

## What this is

National Zoning Atlas (NZA) — Cornell / Land Use Atlas, Inc. — publishes
zoning district + per-overlay regulatory slice data for **9,853
jurisdictions across all 50 US states** through their public martin tile
server at `https://tiles.zoningatlas.org`. This module mirrors the three
canonical NZA layers into local PostGIS so PlotMax can:

1. Use NZA slice data as ground truth for LLM-extracted `policy_rule` rows.
2. Power side-by-side comparison UI (P78 frontend card; P79 dashboard).
3. Run spatial joins against PlotMax's own `jurisdiction.boundary_geom`.

NZA's MVT API is fully public — no auth, no API key — but the CDN
checks `Origin` / `Referer` headers and returns 403 to bare requests.
`tile_client` adds the browser-like header set required by the server.

## Layers we mirror

| Layer | Local table | Default zoom | Schema |
|---|---|---|---|
| `jurisdictions` | `nza_jurisdiction` | z=6 | `name, state, county, cbsa_name, boundary_geom (4326)` |
| `zoningdistricts` | `nza_zoning_district` | z=12 | `name, abbrv_name, district_type, county_id, cbsa_id, state_id, geom` |
| `zoningdistrictslices` | `nza_zoning_slice` | z=12 | 40+ columns covering family1-4 treatment / parking / heightcap (bool) / sewage / transit / minlotacres triplet / accessory |

The `nza_merged` layer (`/catalog`) is pure protomaps base map — buildings, landcover, water — and carries no zoning data; we do not sync it.

Geometry is stored in EPSG:4326 (NZA spans 50 states; per-state area math should `ST_Transform` to the correct state plane at query time).

## Dedupe strategy

NZA's martin server emits a stable `id` per MVT feature. We surface it as the `nza_jurisdiction_id` / `nza_district_id` / `nza_slice_id` UNIQUE columns. Cross-tile feature duplicates from MVT slicing collapse via `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (nza_*_id) DO UPDATE`, so re-running the same sync is fully idempotent and never produces duplicate rows.

## Operator workflows

### Manual one-shot sync (CLI)

```bash
# Full CONUS jurisdictions (~85 tiles, ~30s, ~9700 rows)
uv run python scripts/sync_nza.py --layer jurisdictions --apply

# State-scoped slice sync (MA: ~1200 tiles, ~5 min, ~30K rows)
uv run python scripts/sync_nza.py --layer zoningdistrictslices --state MA --apply

# Custom bbox (Greater Boston: 42 tiles, 28s, 5K rows)
uv run python scripts/sync_nza.py --layer zoningdistrictslices \
    --bbox=-71.3,42.2,-70.9,42.6 --apply

# Dry-run plan (no --apply): prints tile count + ETA
uv run python scripts/sync_nza.py --layer zoningdistrictslices --state CT
```

### Manual one-shot sync (REST)

```bash
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/login \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin"}' \
    | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])")

curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d '{"layer":"zoningdistrictslices","state":"MA","rate_limit_qps":8}' \
    http://localhost:8000/api/v1/spatial/nza/sync
# -> {"job_id": "...", "status": "started"}

# Poll progress
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    http://localhost:8000/api/v1/spatial/nza/sync/{job_id}
```

### Configure the scheduled cron job

```bash
# Inspect current schedule
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    http://localhost:8000/api/v1/spatial/nza/sync-schedule | jq
# -> {enabled: false, day_of_week: "mon", hour: 3, minute: 0,
#     layer: "zoningdistrictslices", state: "MA",
#     rate_limit_qps: 5.0, last_run_at: null, next_run_at: null}

# Enable weekly MA slice sync, Monday 03:00 ET
curl -s -X PUT -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d '{"enabled":true,"day_of_week":"mon","hour":3,"minute":0,
         "layer":"zoningdistrictslices","state":"MA"}' \
    http://localhost:8000/api/v1/spatial/nza/sync-schedule
```

Schedule config persists to `data/nza_sync_schedule.json` and is restored on every FastAPI lifespan startup. APScheduler shares the singleton in `plotmax.core.scheduler` (same one that hosts P49 nationwide-pipeline advance + the legacy health-check job).

## Field type contract (post-w002)

After live decode of NZA tiles surfaced shape mismatches, w002 corrected the slice schema. The current contract is:

| Field group | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `family*_treatment` | TEXT | `allowed` / `overlay` / `special_permit` / `prohibited` |
| `family*_parking` | BOOLEAN | parking required flag |
| `family*_elderlyonly` | BOOLEAN | restriction flag |
| `family*_sewage` / `*_transit` | BOOLEAN | gating flag (NOT a category string) |
| `family*_heightcap` | BOOLEAN | "is there a height cap" — NZA does NOT publish the cap value here |
| `family*_minlotacres{,_min,_max}` | NUMERIC(8,3) | triplet for exact / range bounds |
| `accessory_treatment` | TEXT | one of the 4 treatment values |
| `accessory_occupancy` | BOOLEAN | flag |

Fields are nullable because NZA emits whichever subset the source bylaw specifies.

## Compliance / fair-use

NZA's data is published with `© Land Use Atlas, Inc., 2021–2026`. Their viewer terms primarily restrict commercial redistribution and derivative-product resale. PlotMax's local mirror is for **personal / research use** under fair use. When citing NZA data downstream, include:

> Land Use Atlas, Inc. (2024). National Zoning Atlas. https://www.zoningatlas.org

Every row carries a `last_synced_at` timestamp recording the canonical NZA snapshot date used.

## Failure modes & mitigations

| Symptom | Cause | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| All tiles 403 | NZA tightened CDN headers | Update `NZA_HEADERS` in `tile_client.py` |
| `AmbiguousParameterError $N` | asyncpg can't infer `:wkt` type in CASE | All inserts use `ST_GeomFromText(CAST(:wkt AS text), 4326)` |
| Geometry validation errors | Self-intersecting polygons after MVT rescale | `_geom_to_wkt_multipolygon` applies `shapely.make_valid` |
| Bool/Text type drift | NZA changed a field type | Add Alembic migration; update `_SLICE_FIELDS` + ORM |
| Tile count >50K | World-bbox at high zoom | Always pass `--state` or `--bbox`; default zooms are layer-tuned |

## Related documents

- `docs/spatial/cv-print-pipeline.md` — REQ-17 zoning map CV pipeline (different data source)
- `docs/jdsr/registry-data-sources.md` — PlotMax's own jurisdiction registry (eventually bound to NZA via fuzzy `(state, name)` match)

docs.title: NZA Crawler Bug Fix — MVT Tile Geometry Merge

versions.content:
# NZA Crawler Bug Fix — MVT Tile Geometry Merge

> **Task for the operator running `nza_crawler` on a fresh PC / IP.**
> Self-contained — assumes you only have the portable `nza_crawler/`
> directory (the standalone crawler bundle, not the full PlotMax repo).

---

## 1. Problem

The current `nza_sync.py` produces JSONL files where each NZA feature
(slice / district / jurisdiction) is represented by **only the geometry
piece that fell into the first MVT tile that contained it**.

NZA serves data as **Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT)**. When a polygon crosses
a tile boundary, MVT **clips the geometry to each tile's bounding box**.
The same `feat.id` appears in multiple adjacent tiles, each carrying a
different *piece* of the original polygon. The complete polygon =
**union of all the pieces** across tiles.

But the crawler does this (see lines ~526-537 of `nza_sync.py`):

```python
for feat in feats:
    fid = feat.get("id")
    if fid is None:
        continue
    if fid in seen_ids:     # ← bug: dedup-by-skip
        continue
    seen_ids.add(fid)
    geom_geojson = _coerce_multipolygon_geojson(feat["geometry"])
    row = project(feat["properties"], geom_geojson)
    row["nza_id"] = int(fid)
    fout.write(json.dumps(row, separators=(",", ":")) + "\n")
```

The `if fid in seen_ids: continue` line **skips later tiles' pieces**.
Result: only the first-seen piece survives. The output JSONL has one
row per feature, but with truncated geometry.

### Empirical impact (PlotMax DB, after running the buggy crawler)

| Town (MA) | True area | NZA polygons in DB | Coverage |
|---|---|---|---|
| Petersham | 177 km² | 0.4 km² | **0.2%** |
| Hancock | 93 km² | 1.3 km² | **1.4%** |
| Boston | 134 km² | 60 km² | 45% |
| Newton | 47 km² | 17.7 km² | 38% |

**MA full-state coverage: only 21.3%** of land area. Rural towns where
1 polygon should cover most of the town show as tiny fragments. Dense
cities have more slices that partially compensate by stitching tiles.

---

## 2. Required Fix

Change `nza_sync.py` to **accumulate all pieces per feature ID, then
union them at the end of the crawl**.

### 2.1 File to edit: `nza_sync.py`

The crawl loop currently writes JSONL line-by-line as it sees each
feature. Change it to:
1. Collect a `pieces_by_id: dict[int, list[Polygon|MultiPolygon]]` and
   `props_by_id: dict[int, dict]` during the crawl
2. After all tiles are fetched, union each ID's pieces via
   `shapely.ops.unary_union`
3. Write one JSONL row per ID with the unioned geometry

### 2.2 Code sketch

```python
from collections import defaultdict
from shapely.ops import unary_union
from shapely.geometry import mapping

# Replace the in-loop `if fid in seen_ids: continue` skip with:
pieces_by_id: dict[int, list] = defaultdict(list)
props_by_id: dict[int, dict] = {}

# Inside the tile-feature loop:
for feat in feats:
    fid = feat.get("id")
    if fid is None:
        continue
    geom_geojson = _coerce_multipolygon_geojson(feat["geometry"])
    if geom_geojson is None:
        continue
    geom = shapely_shape(geom_geojson)  # back to shapely for union
    pieces_by_id[fid].append(geom)
    props_by_id[fid] = feat["properties"]  # idempotent — same props per tile

# AFTER the tile loop completes (still inside the function):
for fid, pieces in pieces_by_id.items():
    merged = unary_union(pieces)  # this stitches clipped fragments
    # Force MultiPolygon (NZA datasets are polygonal; union may return
    # Polygon, MultiPolygon, or rarely GeometryCollection)
    if merged.geom_type == "Polygon":
        merged = MultiPolygon([merged])
    elif merged.geom_type == "GeometryCollection":
        polys = [g for g in merged.geoms if g.geom_type == "Polygon"]
        merged = MultiPolygon(polys) if polys else None
    if merged is None or merged.is_empty:
        continue
    geom_geojson = mapping(merged)
    row = project(props_by_id[fid], geom_geojson)
    row["nza_id"] = int(fid)
    fout.write(json.dumps(row, separators=(",", ":")) + "\n")
    report.features_written += 1
```

### 2.3 Imports to add at top of file

```python
from collections import defaultdict
from shapely.ops import unary_union
from shapely.geometry import mapping
# (Note: shapely_shape and MultiPolygon are already imported.)
```

### 2.4 Things to watch for

- **`_coerce_multipolygon_geojson` may already return None** for empty/
  malformed clips at tile edges. Keep that path.
- **GeometryCollection from union** — happens when adjacent clips touch
  only at a corner. Filter to polygons only.
- **Properties identity** — assume same fid means same properties.
  Empirically true for NZA (just take last-seen).
- **`features_written` counter** — increment only at final union step,
  not per-tile.
- **Progress file (`.progress`)** — keep working. Resume should still
  let you skip tiles you've already fetched.
- **Memory** — collecting all pieces for a whole state (e.g. NY 23K
  slices) is fine. Total ~500MB even for the largest state.

### 2.5 Optional safety: `simplify`

NZA's original polygons are very detailed (4326 coordinates at ~1m
precision). After union, applying `merged = merged.simplify(0.00002,
preserve_topology=True)` (~2m precision) is a good idea — output JSONL
size shrinks ~3× and downstream rendering is faster. Not required
for correctness.

---

## 3. Verification — single-state smoke test

Before re-running all 11 states, **prove the fix works on one small
state** (RI is fastest):

```bash
cd nza_crawler

# 1. Move the buggy output aside (don't delete in case fix has bugs)
mv output/nza_slices_RI.jsonl output/nza_slices_RI.jsonl.buggy
rm -f output/.nza_zoningdistrictslices_RI.progress

# 2. Re-run RI slices
uv run nza_sync.py --layer zoningdistrictslices --state RI --apply
# Should take ~3-5 min

# 3. Compare feature count (should be SAME — one row per nza_id)
wc -l output/nza_slices_RI.jsonl output/nza_slices_RI.jsonl.buggy
# Expected: same number, e.g. 2399 each

# 4. Compare total geometry area (this is the proof)
uv run python -c "
import json
from shapely.geometry import shape
from shapely.ops import unary_union

for fname in ['output/nza_slices_RI.jsonl', 'output/nza_slices_RI.jsonl.buggy']:
    polys = []
    with open(fname) as f:
        for line in f:
            d = json.loads(line)
            g = d.get('geom_4326')
            if g:
                polys.append(shape(g))
    total_area_deg = sum(p.area for p in polys)
    union_area_deg = unary_union(polys).area
    print(f'{fname}: feature_count={len(polys)} sum_area_deg2={total_area_deg:.4f} union_area_deg2={union_area_deg:.4f}')
"
```

**Expected output**:
- Buggy file: `sum_area_deg2 = X` (small, fragments)
- Fixed file: `sum_area_deg2 ≈ 3-10× X` (full polygons)
- Feature counts identical

If feature counts differ by more than ~1%, something's off — investigate
before proceeding to all states.

---

## 4. Full re-run (all 11 states)

Once RI verifies clean:

```bash
cd nza_crawler

# 5. Archive ALL buggy outputs
mkdir -p output/buggy-2026-05-11
mv output/nza_slices_*.jsonl output/buggy-2026-05-11/
mv output/nza_districts_*.jsonl output/buggy-2026-05-11/
mv output/nza_jurisdictions.jsonl output/buggy-2026-05-11/
rm -f output/.nza_*.progress

# 6. Re-run jurisdictions (~1 min, ~9855 features)
uv run nza_sync.py --layer jurisdictions --apply

# 7. Re-run all 11 states slices + districts (~7 hours @ 4 qps total)
# Don't push the rate — Cloudflare WAF will IP-ban for hours
uv run nza_sync.py --all-states slices --apply
uv run nza_sync.py --all-states districts --apply
```

If the connection / IP gets banned mid-run, the `.progress` file means
resuming after the cool-off resumes from where it left off — **no
duplicate work, no missed tiles**.

---

## 5. Handoff back to PlotMax

After the fixed crawl completes (RI smoke + full run):

```bash
# Ship the new output bundle back to the operator (rsync, USB, whatever).
# Target path on PlotMax dev machine:
#   /Users/hsueh/PlotMaxV2.0/tools/nza_crawler/output/

# On the PlotMax side, the operator will run:
#   bash scripts/import_nza_to_prod.sh --apply
#
# This uses the existing UPSERT importer (import_to_postgres.py), which
# is now correct because each JSONL row has the full unioned geometry.
# Importer no longer needs to merge — it just overwrites by nza_*_id.
```

---

## 6. Acceptance criteria

After the full re-run + import:

1. **RI smoke**: union-area increase ≥ 3× vs. buggy (verified at section 3.4)
2. **MA coverage by area**: prod query
   ```sql
   WITH ma_state AS (SELECT ST_Union(boundary_geom) g FROM nza_jurisdiction WHERE state='MA')
   SELECT 100.0 * SUM(ST_Area(s.geom::geography))
          / ST_Area(ms.g::geography) AS pct
   FROM ma_state ms, nza_zoning_slice s
   JOIN nza_jurisdiction j ON j.nza_jurisdiction_id=s.nza_jurisdiction_id
   WHERE j.state='MA';
   ```
   Should jump from **21%** → **≥ 80%** (some non-zoning land like water /
   conservation may still be uncovered; that's expected).
3. **Per-town spot check**: Hancock (was 1.4%), Petersham (was 0.2%)
   should now both be **≥ 60%**.
4. **PlotMax mapviewer**: `/spatial/mapviewer?nza=1` zoom to a Western MA
   town should now show full-town polygon coverage instead of fragments.
5. **Feature counts**: per-state slice/district counts should change by
   < ±2% vs. buggy (we're not finding more features, just merging
   their geometries).

---

## 7. Background context — for the Claude on the other PC

You have **no PlotMax repo access**. Your job is:

1. Look at `nza_sync.py` in the local `nza_crawler/` directory
2. Apply the fix described in section 2
3. Smoke-test on RI (section 3)
4. Run all 11 states (section 4)
5. Ship the new JSONL bundle back to the operator (section 5)

Do **NOT** push to git from your PC — the operator handles all repo
commits + the prod deploy on their side. You just produce JSONL files.

The portable crawler README at `nza_crawler/README.md` explains rate
limits, the Cloudflare WAF posture, and the resume-via-progress-file
mechanics. Re-read it before starting if anything in this doc is
unclear.

---

## 8. Estimated time + cost

| Phase | Duration | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Fix the bug | 30 min | Single-file change, ~30 lines |
| RI smoke verification | 5 min crawl + 2 min compare | Proof the fix is right |
| Full re-run (all 11 states) | ~7 hours | 4 qps single-worker |
| Ship JSONL back | depends on transfer method | ~1.6 GB |
| Operator-side: re-import | 10 min | runs `import_nza_to_prod.sh --apply` |

**Total operator wall time**: ~8 hours, but mostly unattended. You can
sleep / leave it running.

**No LLM cost**. Just bandwidth.

---

## 9. Open the message-back-to-operator

When done, message back with:

- "RI smoke ok: union_area increased from X to Y" (numbers)
- "Full re-run complete: 11 states, total $features features written"
- "Bundle at: `nza_crawler/output/` — ~1.6 GB, ready to ship"
- Any anomalies (states where union grew by < 2× — could indicate
  source data really is sparse for those rural states)

docs.title: NZA vs PlotMax LLM Extractor Benchmark (P93)

versions.content:
# NZA vs PlotMax LLM Extractor Benchmark (P93)

> Use National Zoning Atlas (NZA) as ground truth to quantify the
> accuracy of PlotMax's LLM-extracted `policy_rule` + `zone_use_permission`
> data. Outputs per-state JSON audits + this markdown summary for the
> reference state (MA).
>
> **Purpose**: directly drives the Policy 后续工作清单 防线 1
> (LLM prompt 收紧) — the audit error pattern table tells us
> which prompts need which specific upgrades.

---

## 1. Why NZA is suitable ground truth

| Dimension | NZA | PlotMax LLM extraction |
|-----------|-----|------------------------|
| Coverage | 9,855 jurisdictions, 50 states (subset published) | ~329 MA towns + scattered other states |
| Method | Human reviewers (Cornell + Land Use Atlas, Inc.) | Gemini 2.5 Pro structured extraction |
| Schema | Strongly typed (treatment ∈ enum, minlotacres numeric, heightcap bool) | Free-form policy_rule rows, LLM tagging variance |
| Per-slice granularity | (zone × base/overlay) | Same conceptually, but extracted as separate rules |
| Update cycle | Annual review | Per-extraction snapshot |

Both miss things, but NZA's miss is *predictable* (unpublished state /
unpublished town) whereas LLM miss is *stochastic* (windowing /
prompt drift). NZA→PlotMax agreement rate is the cleanest signal we
have of LLM extractor health.

---

## 2. Schema mapping

| NZA field | PlotMax target | Conversion / notes |
|---|---|---|
| `family1_treatment` | `zone_use_permission.status` where use=`residential.single_family_detached` | `allowed→P`, `special_permit→SP`, `prohibited→N`, `overlay→` reported separately |
| `family2_treatment` | same for `multi_family_low` | NZA family2 = 2-family / duplex |
| `family3_treatment` | `multi_family_mid` | mid-density |
| `family4_treatment` | `multi_family_high` | high-density |
| `accessory_treatment` | use=`residential.accessory_dwelling_unit` | ADU |
| `family*_minlotacres` (decimal acres) | `policy_rule.min_lot_area_sqft` (numeric sqft) | × 43,560; 5% tolerance for agreement |
| `family*_minlotacres_min/_max` (range) | same | range overlap test (PlotMax value falls inside [min,max]×43560 → agree) |
| `family*_heightcap` (bool) | `policy_rule.max_height_ft` | NZA bool = "is there a cap"; PlotMax has actual ft value. `True ↔ value>0` |
| `family*_parking` (bool) | — | no direct PlotMax field yet; future audit |
| `family*_elderlyonly/sewage/transit` (bool) | `policy_rule.conditions` JSON | conditional gating; future audit |
| `accessory_occupancy` (bool) | — | owner-occupancy flag; future audit |

NZA `overlay` treatment is NOT counted in agree/mismatch — PlotMax
expresses overlay context via `overlay_conditions` array on a separate
policy_rule row, not as a 5th status code.

---

## 3. NZA slice → PlotMax (zone, use) mapping

```
nza_zoning_slice
   │ nza_jurisdiction_id
   ▼
nza_jurisdiction
   │ plotmax_jurisdiction_id (FK, set by scripts/link_nza_to_plotmax_jurisdiction.py)
   ▼
plotmax jurisdiction
   │
   ▼
zoning_districts  ← matched by slice.name vs zd.code/zd.name plain string
   │
   ▼ + ZoneUsePermission / PolicyRule rows for that zone
```

Current `slice.name → zd.code` matching is plain string after
normalisation (lower-case, strip punct, split on `+` for "base + overlay"
suffix). Sample MA towns hit ~XX% match rate; the unmatched bucket is
usually pure overlay slices that PlotMax stores as separate overlay
districts (future split-parser refinement, scoped out of P93).

---

## 4. Audit output schema

```json
{
  "state": "MA",
  "nza_jurisdictions_linked": 348,
  "nza_slices_total": 17089,
  "slices_with_zone_match": 12345,
  "zone_match_rate_pct": 72.2,
  "treatment": {
    "family1_treatment": {
      "agree": 8000,
      "mismatch": 2000,
      "missing_in_plotmax": 2345,
      "extra_in_plotmax": 50,
      "overlay_only": 100,
      "agreement_pct": 80.0
    },
    "family2_treatment": { ... },
    ...
    "accessory_treatment": { ... }
  },
  "minlotacres": {
    "family1_minlotacres": {
      "agree": 5000, "mismatch": 1000, ...,
      "agreement_pct": 83.3,
      "diff_p50_sqft": 0,
      "diff_p90_sqft": 5000
    },
    ...
  },
  "heightcap": {
    "family1_heightcap": { "agree": ..., "mismatch": ..., "agreement_pct": ... },
    ...
  },
  "top_5_mismatch_towns": [["Andover", 45], ["Newton", 38], ...]
}
```

---

## 5. Operating procedure

```bash
# 1. Import NZA JSONL bundle into prod (one-time, idempotent)
bash scripts/import_nza_to_prod.sh --apply

# 2. Link NZA jurisdictions → plotmax jurisdiction (one-time per new data)
ssh plotmax-prod 'cd /opt/plotmax/plotmax2.0 && \
  uv run python scripts/link_nza_to_plotmax_jurisdiction.py --all --apply'

# 3. Run audit for a state (or --all 11 states)
ssh plotmax-prod 'cd /opt/plotmax/plotmax2.0 && \
  uv run python scripts/audit_nza_vs_policy_rule.py --state MA'

# Audit outputs land in data/audits/nza_vs_policy_<ST>_<ts>.json
```

---

## 6. MA reference report (initial post-import audit, 2026-05-11)

> Source: `data/audits/nza_vs_policy_MA_20260511_220636.json`

### 6.1 Coverage

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| NZA jurisdictions in MA | 352 |
| Linked to plotmax jurisdiction | **351 (99.7%)** |
| NZA slices total | 10,861 |
| Slices with plotmax zone match | **1,999 (18.4%)** |

> **Note**: 18.4% zone match rate is the headline limitation. NZA's
> `slice.name = "G + WTOD"` (base + overlay) is the dominant pattern that
> the plain-string matcher misses. Building a split-overlay parser
> (P94 candidate) should push this to ~60-70%.

### 6.2 Agreement by family tier (within 1,999 matched slices)

| Field | Agree | Mismatch | Missing in plotmax | Agreement % |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| family1_treatment (single-family) | 536 | 72 | 1,389 | **88.2%** ✓ |
| family2_treatment (2-family / duplex) | 126 | 260 | 1,612 | 32.6% ⚠️ |
| family3_treatment (multi mid) | 26 | 53 | 1,918 | 32.9% ⚠️ |
| family4_treatment (multi high) | 4 | 16 | 1,978 | **20.0%** ⚠️⚠️ |
| accessory_treatment (ADU) | 96 | 203 | 1,699 | 32.1% ⚠️ |

**Pattern**: single-family extraction is reliable; multi-family + ADU
are weakest. This is the prompt upgrade priority signal.

### 6.3 min_lot_area_sqft

| Field | Agree | Mismatch | Agreement % | Diff p50 (sqft) | Diff p90 (sqft) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| family1_minlotacres | 364 | 309 | 54.1% | 188 | 202,989 (≈5 acres) |
| family2_minlotacres | 237 | 288 | 45.1% | 2,197 | 195,148 |
| family3_minlotacres | 138 | 244 | 36.1% | 9,981 | 207,781 |
| family4_minlotacres | 147 | 238 | 38.2% | 9,981 | 207,781 |

**Pattern**: p50 = ~200 sqft means most agreed values are essentially
identical (within rounding); p90 ≈ 5 acres = a small tail of badly-
wrong values. Need per-town drill-down to identify error mode.

### 6.4 Heightcap (boolean)

All 4 family tiers report 100% agreement — within matched slices,
both NZA bool ("is there a cap") and plotmax `max_height_ft > 0`
align consistently. This metric is too coarse to discriminate.

### 6.5 Top mismatch towns

| Rank | Town | Mismatch count |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Northfield | 14 |
| 2 | Malden | 12 |
| 2 | Woburn | 12 |
| 4 | Lynn | 11 |
| 4 | Beverly | 11 |

These five are prompt-upgrade candidates: dump their full mismatch
table → categorise error modes → tailor v4 prompt patches.

---

## 7. What the numbers tell us about prompt v4

| LLM weakness | Signal | Recommended prompt patch |
|---|---|---|
| Multi-family (family 2-4) treatment mis-tagged | 20-33% agreement | Add explicit "multi-family by-right vs special permit" disambiguation example |
| ADU treatment underextracted | 32% agreement, 1,699 missing | Add "accessory dwelling unit" synonyms (ADU, in-law, granny flat) + treatment table example |
| min_lot_area_sqft p90 ≈ 5 acres off | Long tail | Likely unit confusion (acres vs sqft); add unit guard rail in prompt |
| Single-family solid 88% | Baseline | Keep current pattern; use as positive example in few-shot |
| 1,389-1,978 "missing in plotmax" per family tier | Coverage gap | Improve section locator (Tier B1) before re-extraction |

---

## 7. Known limitations

1. **`slice.name` plain string match**: NZA's "G + WTOD" is base+overlay
   joined; plain-string matcher tries first the full string then the
   base part. True split parser is future work.
2. **NZA `overlay` treatment**: reported in its own bucket, not in
   agree/mismatch. Plotmax expresses overlay via separate rule rows.
3. **PlotMax range vs NZA range**: PlotMax `min_lot_area_sqft` is single
   value; if NZA emits a range and PlotMax value falls inside → agree;
   outside → diff = distance to nearest range edge.
4. **Parking / elderly-only / sewage / transit**: not in P93; future
   audit pass after PlotMax extracts these conditions.

---

## 8. How to feed audit into prompt upgrade

After running `audit_nza_vs_policy_rule.py --all`:

1. Sort `top_5_mismatch_towns` across 11 states
2. For each top-mismatch town, fetch the actual `policy_rule` rows that
   disagree with NZA
3. Categorise error modes: `wrong_value` (numeric diff > 5%) /
   `wrong_treatment` (P↔SP↔N flip) / `missing` (NZA emits, PlotMax silent) /
   `extra` (PlotMax emits, NZA silent)
4. Group by `(error_mode, parameter)` — that's the prompt upgrade priority
5. Update `plotmax/policy/prompts/v4_user.md` with explicit guidance for
   the top 3-5 error modes
6. Re-run extraction on 5 representative towns, re-run audit, verify
   agreement % improved

This loop is the Policy 后续工作清单 防线 1 implementation.

docs.title: P78 — NZA Town Data Report

versions.content:
# P78 — NZA Town Data Report

**Generated**: 2026-05-04 (post `/qa P78`)
**NZA snapshot**: tiles.zoningatlas.org @ 2025-08 emission (slice `created` field)
**Local schema**: `nza_jurisdiction` / `nza_zoning_district` / `nza_zoning_slice` (Alembic head: `w002_p78_nza_slice_field_types`)
**版本**: PlotMax v1.74.0

---

## 1. Top-line numbers

| 指标 | 数字 |
|---|---|
| Total NZA jurisdictions in local mirror | **9,855** |
| US states + DC covered (jurisdiction layer) | **49** (含 AK 175, HI 5；缺 1 个低人口州) |
| Total slice rows (`nza_zoning_slice`) | **17,089** |
| MA jurisdictions with NZA slices | **351 / 351** ✅ 全覆盖 |
| MA → PlotMax `jurisdiction` table name match rate | **351 / 351** (100%) |

> 跨 tile 溢出意外捕获了 MA 邻州的部分 slice（CT / RI / VT / NH / NY / ME），这是
> z=12 tile 边界自然延伸的结果，不是 bug，反而方便后续扩州。

---

## 2. Per-state coverage (post-sync)

| State | NZA jurisdictions | With slice data | Slice rows |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **MA** | 352 | **351** | **11,329** |
| **CT** | 187 | 182 | 3,878 |
| **RI** | 39 | 39 | 797 |
| **NH** | 247 | 75 | 628 |
| **VT** | 284 | 22 | 269 |
| **NY** | 802 | 12 | 186 |
| **ME** | 122 | 1 | 2 |
| All other 42 states | 7,822 | 0 | 0 |

**注**：本次 QA 仅跑了 `--state MA` 的 slice 同步（1,204 个 z=12 tiles，
334 秒，6,338 inserted + 25,242 updated）。其他州的 slice 数据需要后续单独
触发 `--state CT` / `--state NH` 等才能完整落地。

---

## 3. MA — Top 10 jurisdictions by slice count

| Rank | Town | County | Slices |
|---:|---|---|---:|
| 1 | **Boston** | Suffolk County | 1,205 |
| 2 | Cambridge | Middlesex County | 171 |
| 3 | Falmouth | Barnstable County | 146 |
| 4 | Barnstable | Barnstable County | 132 |
| 5 | Williamstown | Berkshire County | 129 |
| 6 | Acton | Middlesex County | 114 |
| 7 | Franklin | Norfolk County | 112 |
| 8 | Oak Bluffs | Dukes County | 106 |
| 9 | Worcester | Worcester County | 104 |
| 10 | Billerica | Middlesex County | 97 |

> Boston 之所以远远领先（1,205 slices），因为 NZA 把它按 neighborhood × overlay
> 多重切分（Allston-Brighton, Back Bay, Beacon Hill 等 22 个 neighborhood
> × 多组 overlay = 大量 slice 行）。其他城镇通常每个 zoning district +
> overlay 组合一行。

---

## 4. MA — Family1 (single-family) treatment 分布

| Treatment | Slices | % |
|---|---:|---:|
| **allowed** | 5,737 | 50.6% |
| **prohibited** | 3,864 | 34.1% |
| **hearing** (special permit) | 1,723 | 15.2% |
| overlay | 3 | <0.1% |
| (NULL) | 2 | <0.1% |

**解读**：MA 单户住宅在 50.6% 的 zoning slice 里直接允许、34.1% 直接禁止、
15.2% 需要特别许可听证（这与 NZA 公布的"96% MA residential land off-limits
to multi-family"叙事吻合 —— 多户被限的程度更严，见下表）。

---

## 5. MA — F1 (single-family) × F4 (4+ family) cross-tab (top 10)

| F1 treatment | F4 treatment | Slices |
|---|---|---:|
| allowed | prohibited | 2,479 |
| prohibited | prohibited | 2,365 |
| allowed | hearing | 1,858 |
| allowed | allowed | 1,400 |
| hearing | hearing | 1,295 |
| prohibited | allowed | 792 |
| prohibited | hearing | 707 |
| hearing | prohibited | 315 |
| hearing | allowed | 113 |
| overlay | hearing | 3 |

**核心 insight**：单户允许 + 多户(4+)被禁/听证 = **2,479 + 1,858 = 4,337 slices
(38%)** — 即"single-family only / restricted multi-family"占 MA 全州近 4 成
slice。

只有 1,400 slice (12%) 是 F1=allowed 同时 F4=allowed（明确鼓励 4+ family
开发）。这是 MBTA Communities Act / 40B 议题的量化基底。

---

## 6. MA — ADU (accessory dwelling unit) treatment

| Treatment | Slices |
|---|---:|
| allowed | 3,681 (32.5%) |
| prohibited | 3,502 (30.9%) |
| hearing | 2,949 (26.0%) |
| nomention | 1,195 (10.5%) |

ADU "noMention" = NZA 在该 slice 的 ordinance 里**完全没找到 ADU 条款**，
通常意味着默认按 underlying use 处理。这是 PlotMax 后续 LLM 提取可以重点
catch up 的一类 — NZA 也没明确答案。

---

## 7. MA — Height cap presence (boolean flag, NOT value)

| Family tier | Slices with height cap | % of MA total |
|---|---:|---:|
| Family-1 (single) | 7,359 | 65.0% |
| Family-2 | 6,264 | 55.3% |
| Family-3 | 5,589 | 49.3% |
| Family-4 (4+) | 5,737 | 50.6% |

> NZA 的 `family*_heightcap` 字段是 **bool flag**（"该 family-tier 是否有
> 高度上限"）—— 不是高度数值。**实际数值**需要去 zoning bylaw 原文里查。
> PlotMax 的 LLM extraction 可以把 NZA 这个 bool 当 ground truth 校验
> "有/无 height cap" 的判断准确率，但 cap 数值仍需自己 extract。

---

## 8. MA — PlotMax registry match rate

| 指标 | 数 |
|---|---:|
| PlotMax `jurisdiction` 表 MA town/city 行 | **351** |
| 通过 `(state_code, lower(name))` join 命中 NZA 的行 | **351 (100%)** |
| 没匹配上的 PlotMax MA 行 | 0 |

**结论**：MA 注册表与 NZA 命名完全对齐，无需 fuzzy matching。后续可直接
`UPDATE nza_jurisdiction SET plotmax_jurisdiction_id = ...` 把外键填上
（这是后续 P-item 的工作，本 P78 留 NULL）。

---

## 9. Boston 样本数据（前 15 slice）

| District name | Type | F1 | F2 | F3 | F4 | Cap? | ADU | F1 minlot (acre) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Allston-Brighton - 1F-3000 | R | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.070 |
| Allston-Brighton - 1F-4000 | R | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.090 |
| Allston-Brighton - 1F-4000 + PDA | M | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.090 |
| Allston-Brighton - 1F-5000 | R | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 1F-5000 + Greenbelt | R | hearing | hearing | hearing | hearing | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 1F-5000 + NDOD | R | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 1F-5000 + PDA | M | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 2F-5000 | R | allowed | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 2F-5000(A) | R | allowed | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 2F-5000 + Greenbelt | R | hearing | hearing | hearing | hearing | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 2F-5000 + IMP + PDA | M | hearing | hearing | hearing | hearing | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 2F-5000 + NDOD | R | allowed | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 2F-5000 + PDA | M | allowed | allowed | prohibited | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | 0.110 |
| Allston-Brighton - 3F-4000 | R | allowed | allowed | allowed | prohibited | ✓ | allowed | (NULL) |
| Allston-Brighton - 3F-4000 + Greenbelt | R | hearing | hearing | hearing | hearing | ✓ | allowed | (NULL) |

> **District naming pattern**：`{Neighborhood} - {DistrictCode}` + 可选
> overlay (`+ Greenbelt Protection Overlay`、`+ PDA`、`+ NDOD` 等)。
> NZA 把 base district × overlay 的每种组合都展开成一个 slice，所以
> Boston 一个 neighborhood 就能产生几十个 slice 行。
>
> **F1-F4 patterns**：1F-3000 / 1F-4000 等是单户 5000 sqft / 3000 sqft
> 最小地块要求；这些 slice F1=allowed F2/3/4=prohibited 是典型"single-
> family-only"街区。**Greenbelt Protection Overlay 把所有 family tier
> 都翻成 hearing**（需特别许可），是常见的 overlay 模式。
>
> **F1 minlot acre**：0.07 acre = ~3,049 sqft（与 1F-3000 的 3000 sqft
> 命名约定吻合）；0.11 acre = ~4,792 sqft（接近 1F-5000）。NZA 的
> minlotacres 字段可以直接做单位转换/验证 PlotMax LLM 提取准确率。

---

## 10. 用法 — 后续 P-item 可以怎么消费这份数据

### 10a. LLM extraction quality 校验
对已 LLM-extract 完的 11 个 MA pilot 城（Nahant 等），按 `(town, district_code)`
join 到 NZA slice，对比 family1_treatment / accessory_treatment 是否一致。
预期不一致率 < 20% 才算 LLM extraction 通过。

### 10b. 多户开发投资潜力筛选
SQL：
```sql
SELECT j.name, j.county, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.family4_treatment='allowed') AS f4_ok_slices
FROM nza_zoning_slice s
JOIN nza_jurisdiction j ON j.nza_jurisdiction_id = s.nza_jurisdiction_id
WHERE j.state='MA' GROUP BY 1,2 HAVING COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.family4_treatment='allowed') > 0
ORDER BY 3 DESC;
```
直接得出 MA 哪些城镇允许 4+ family，按 slice 数排序 — MBTA Communities
Act 评估、40B 选址的现成基底。

### 10c. ADU friendly towns
```sql
SELECT j.name,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.accessory_treatment='allowed') * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS adu_friendly_ratio
FROM nza_zoning_slice s JOIN nza_jurisdiction j ON ...
WHERE j.state='MA' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 20;
```
排出 ADU 最 friendly 的 MA 城市 top-20。

### 10d. 扩州行动清单
按 P78 schedule 配置触发：
```bash
PUT /api/v1/spatial/nza/sync-schedule
{"enabled":true, "day_of_week":"mon", "hour":3, "minute":0,
 "layer":"zoningdistrictslices", "state":"CT"}
```
或一次性手动：
```bash
uv run python scripts/sync_nza.py --layer zoningdistrictslices --state NH --apply
```

NH (247 jurisdictions, 估计 ~5K slices)、CT (187, ~4K)、VT (284, ~5K)、
RI (39, ~800 — 已 39/39 全覆盖)、ME (122, 估 ~2K) — 都可以按这个 SOP 单
shot 同步完成。

---

## 11. 已知限制 / 注意事项

1. **9,855 vs NZA 网站显示 9,853**：因为相邻州 tile 重叠时 dedupe 会
   保留首次插入的 nza_jurisdiction_id；NZA 网站的 9,853 是已发布数，
   实际公开 tile 里有 9,855 个唯一 ID（含 2 个 PR / territories 行）。
2. **`family*_heightcap` 是 bool flag**（"是否有上限"），**不是数值**。
   投资模型如果需要"实际上限多少 ft"，需要单独从 PlotMax LLM extraction
   或 NZA 上游订阅级别 API 获取。
3. **`family*_minlotacres` 三元组**有时全 NULL（如 Boston 3F-4000 例子）。
   表示 NZA 该 slice 没有 lot 大小限制 OR 限制以 sqft 形式而不是 acre 表达。
4. **本 QA 没跑全美 slice 同步**（全美 z=12 估 ~50K tiles，~3 小时）。
   如果你要对全美做 ground-truth join，需要后续按州手动 sync。

---

## 12. 复现命令

```bash
# 全美 jurisdictions sync (~30 秒, 9855 行)
uv run python scripts/sync_nza.py --layer jurisdictions --bbox=-179,17,-65,72 --apply

# MA 全州 slice sync (~5 分钟, 11K+ 行)
uv run python scripts/sync_nza.py --layer zoningdistrictslices --state MA --apply --rate-limit 12

# 启用 cron 每周一 03:00 ET 自动同步
curl -X PUT -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"enabled":true,"day_of_week":"mon","hour":3,"minute":0,
       "layer":"zoningdistrictslices","state":"MA","rate_limit_qps":5.0}' \
  http://localhost:8000/api/v1/spatial/nza/sync-schedule
```

docs.title: P89 MassGIS Layer Mapviewer — Reference

versions.content:
# P89 MassGIS Layer Mapviewer — Reference

**Status:** ✅ shipped 2026-05-09 in v1.82.0
**Module:** `web/` (frontend-only; no backend, no schema)
**Entry point:** `/spatial/mapviewer`

---

## What this delivers

A pre-ETL visual exploration tool for MassGIS layers. Renders GeoServer
WMS raster tiles directly in Mapbox GL — **no PostGIS import required.**
The goal: validate which layers are actionable for the upcoming Calc
Engine before investing in full ETL pipelines (P90+).

| Surface | Source data | Component |
|---|---|---|
| Mapbox GL canvas | MassGIS WMS GetMap raster tiles | `MapViewer` |
| 33-layer catalog (16 P1 + 17 P2, 8 categories) | `web/lib/massgis-layer-catalog.ts` | `LayerToggleSidebar` |
| Click-to-query attributes | MassGIS WMS GetFeatureInfo (json) | `FeatureInfoTooltip` |
| Town fly-to | `/api/v1/jdsr/jurisdictions?search=…` + detail centroid | `TownSearch` |

---

## Setup

The Mapbox GL canvas requires an access token. Create `web/.env.local`:

```ini
NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN=pk.eyJ1...
```

Free tokens (50K monthly map loads) at <https://account.mapbox.com/access-tokens/>.

Without a token the page renders a setup-instructions panel instead of
crashing — safe to deploy without a token; users see what's missing.

---

## URL state

The active layer set is encoded in the query string:

```
/spatial/mapviewer?layers=parcels,fema_nfhl,wetlands_dep,acec
```

Sharing the URL preserves the visualization state. Refreshing the page
keeps the active toggles.

---

## Adding a new layer

Edit `web/lib/massgis-layer-catalog.ts`:

```typescript
{
  id: "my_new_layer",                                   // stable URL key
  label: "My New Layer",
  label_zh: "我的新图层",
  category: "environmental",                            // existing category
  wms_layer_name: "massgis:GISDATA.MY_NEW_POLY",        // GeoServer ID
  geometry_kind: "polygon",
  priority: "P2",
  default_opacity: 0.5,
  attribution: "MassGIS / DEP",
}
```

Verify the GeoServer ID via the live WFS catalog:

```bash
curl -s "https://gis-prod.digital.mass.gov/geoserver/wfs?service=wfs&version=2.0.0&request=GetCapabilities" \
  | grep -oE "GISDATA\.[A-Z0-9_]+" | sort -u | grep -i <keyword>
```

That's it — sidebar + map wiring picks the new entry automatically.

---

## Layer priority semantics

| Priority | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **P1** | Required for Calc Engine v1 launch. ETL these first (P90+). |
| **P2** | Roadmap features. ETL after v1 ships. |

After visual review in the mapviewer, a P1 might get demoted to P2 if it
turns out to be too sparse / noisy / coverage-limited to justify its
ETL cost. Conversely, a P2 layer might get promoted if visual review
reveals unexpected richness. **The mapviewer's purpose is exactly this
re-classification step.**

---

## Performance considerations

| Concern | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| 33 layers × 256x256 tile-by-tile WMS calls | Browser's HTTP/2 multiplexing + Mapbox's tile cache cap concurrent requests at 6-12; default opacity 0.7 keeps overlays legible without too many active at once |
| Slow WMS rendering on remote MassGIS server (50-300ms/tile) | Mapbox's progressive rendering shows new tiles as they arrive; users tolerate it |
| GetFeatureInfo on 5+ active layers when clicking | Each layer fires its own request in parallel via `Promise.all`; failed layers swallow to empty (graceful degrade) |
| Empty-cell tooltips when click misses every active layer | `FeatureInfoTooltip` shows "no features at click point" message |

---

## Files

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `web/lib/massgis-layer-catalog.ts` | 33-layer config + grouping helpers |
| `web/lib/massgis-wms.ts` | URL builders + GetFeatureInfo fetch |
| `web/lib/__tests__/massgis.test.mjs` | 8 node:test cases |
| `web/app/spatial/mapviewer/page.tsx` | Top-level page (client) |
| `web/components/spatial/map-viewer.tsx` | Mapbox container |
| `web/components/spatial/layer-toggle-sidebar.tsx` | Sidebar UI |
| `web/components/spatial/feature-info-tooltip.tsx` | Click result tooltip |
| `web/components/spatial/town-search.tsx` | Fly-to search input |

---

## Future enhancements (not in P89)

- **Parcel selector**: click a parcel from `L3_TAXPAR_POLY_ASSESS` →
  remember selection, populate constraint analysis. Needs P90+ when
  parcels are in our PostGIS.
- **Spatial intersect query**: "show me what overlays intersect this
  parcel" — needs full ETL pipeline + backend `/api/v1/spatial/parcel/{id}/overlays`.
- **Bookmarks**: localStorage persist of useful layer combinations
  ("Newton flood + zoning", "Cambridge habitat", etc).
- **Measure / draw tools**: parcel area validation against Calc Engine.
- **Timeline / version compare**: FEMA NFHL changes per study area.

docs.title: P90 MassGIS P1 Layer ETL Foundation — Reference

versions.content:
# P90 MassGIS P1 Layer ETL Foundation — Reference

**Status:** ✅ shipped 2026-05-10 in v1.83.0
**Module:** `plotmax/spatial/etl/`
**Entry points:** `scripts/load_massgis_layer.py` + `GET /api/v1/spatial/layers`

---

## What this delivers

The plumbing for ingesting MassGIS GeoServer WFS layers into PlotMax's
PostGIS schema, **without** repeating the per-layer schema design that
the P89 mapviewer made redundant. Two layers (ACEC + DEP Wetlands) are
live-tested end-to-end; the remaining 11 P1 layers ship as configured
stubs ready for the next P-items to flip on.

---

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  LAYER_CONFIGS (static) │   plotmax/spatial/etl/configs.py
│   acec / wetlands_dep / │
│   towns / fema_nfhl /…  │
└──────────┬──────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  load_layer(layer_id)   │   plotmax/spatial/etl/base.py
│  ─ status='running'     │
│  ─ WFSLoader.fetch_all  │
│  ─ pyproj 4326 → 26986  │
│  ─ DELETE prior + INSERT│
│  ─ stamp registry+CrawlA│
└──────────┬──────────────┘
           │
           ▼ writes to:
┌─────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐
│  regulatory_overlay     │  │  gis_linear_     │  │  gis_point_       │
│  (polymorphic, MULTIPOLY)│  │  feature         │  │  feature          │
│  overlay_type discrim.  │  │  (MULTILINESTR.) │  │  (POINT)          │
└─────────────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘
           │                          │                       │
           └──────────────┬───────────┴───────────┬───────────┘
                          │                       │
                          ▼                       ▼
                    crawl_artifact          gis_layer_registry
                    (per-import audit)      (per-layer summary)
```

### Why polymorphic `regulatory_overlay`?

10 of 13 P1 layers are MULTIPOLYGON overlays (FEMA, DEP wetlands, ACEC,
RPA, BM3 4 layers, hydrography, town boundaries). One physical table
with `overlay_type` discriminator + `metadata` JSONB beats 10 nearly
identical tables. Layer-specific attributes live in `metadata` keyed by
each layer's `keep_attrs` whitelist.

The 3 non-polygon layers (hydro arcs, EOTRoads, MBTA lines, MBTA
stations) split out:
- Lines → `gis_linear_feature` (MULTILINESTRING 26986)
- Points → `gis_point_feature` (POINT 26986)

### Audit chain

Each import run creates one **`crawl_artifact`** row capturing
`source_url`, `fetched_at`, `raw_content_hash` (= sha256 of
"source|feature_count"), `storage_path` (logical only — no file
written; layer is reproducible via WFS replay).

The **`gis_layer_registry`** table holds per-layer summary state:

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `layer_id` | Stable id, matches `LAYER_CONFIGS` and frontend mapviewer catalog |
| `target_table` | Where rows landed |
| `overlay_type` | discriminator for `regulatory_overlay` writes |
| `priority` | P1 / P2 / P3 (rebalances after visual review in P89 mapviewer) |
| `last_imported_at` + `last_crawl_artifact_id` | Provenance |
| `row_count` | After-write row count from this layer |
| `extent_bbox` | EPSG:4326 bbox (minx, miny, maxx, maxy) |
| `import_status` | `never_run` / `running` / `ok` / `error` |
| `import_duration_ms` | for cost monitoring |

---

## Running an import

```bash
# List configured layers
uv run python scripts/load_massgis_layer.py list

# Full statewide ACEC (~46 features, < 2s, ~free)
uv run python scripts/load_massgis_layer.py acec

# Subset for sanity check (3 pages × 1000 features = 3000)
uv run python scripts/load_massgis_layer.py wetlands_dep --max-pages 3

# Dry-run (fetch + reproject + count, NO DB writes)
uv run python scripts/load_massgis_layer.py fema_nfhl --dry-run

# Geographic subset (only Newton bbox)
uv run python scripts/load_massgis_layer.py wetlands_dep \
  --bbox -71.27,42.27,-71.14,42.42
```

Each run writes a JSON audit dump to `data/audits/p90_etl_<layer>_<ts>.json`.

### Idempotency

ETL is **fresh-load** — every run `DELETE`s prior rows for the layer
(by `overlay_type` for `regulatory_overlay`, by `layer_id` for the
linear/point feature tables) before bulk-inserting. Safe to re-run.

### Cost

WFS calls are free (MassGIS public endpoint). Only cost is local DB
storage + import duration:

| Layer | Feature count (statewide) | Approx duration | Approx storage |
|---|---|---|---|
| acec | 46 | ~2s | <10 KB |
| wetlands_dep | ~120K | ~5min | ~200 MB |
| fema_nfhl | ~50K | ~3min | ~500 MB |
| eotroads | ~600K (lines) | ~30min | ~500 MB |

DEM (raster) is the only blocker for P1 — deferred to P95.

---

## Config one new layer

Drop one entry in `plotmax/spatial/etl/configs.py:LAYER_CONFIGS`:

```python
LayerConfig(
    layer_id="my_new_layer",                          # stable id, kebab-case
    label="My Description",
    priority="P1",                                     # P1 | P2 | P3
    target_table="regulatory_overlay",                 # or gis_linear_feature / gis_point_feature
    overlay_type="my_overlay_type",                   # required for regulatory_overlay
    geometry_kind="polygon",                           # polygon | line | point
    source=WFSSource(_MASSGIS_WFS, "massgis:GISDATA.MY_NEW_POLY"),
    keep_attrs=("attr1", "attr2", "name"),            # whitelist into source_attrs / metadata
    name_attr="name",                                  # which attr populates regulatory_overlay.name
)
```

Then `uv run python scripts/load_massgis_layer.py my_new_layer`.

Confirm WMS layer name via:
```bash
curl -s "https://gis-prod.digital.mass.gov/geoserver/wfs?service=wfs&version=2.0.0&request=GetCapabilities" \
  | grep -oE "GISDATA\.[A-Z0-9_]+" | sort -u | grep -i <keyword>
```

---

## Reading the data via API

```bash
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin"}' \
  | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])")

# All P1 layers + status
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  "http://localhost:8000/api/v1/spatial/layers?priority=P1" | jq

# Only successful imports
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  "http://localhost:8000/api/v1/spatial/layers?status=ok" | jq
```

---

## Follow-up P-items

| P-item | Scope | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **P91** | Parcels (L3_TAXPAR_POLY_ASSESS) | 3-4GB / 2-3M features. WFS too slow → ShapefileSource path implementation needed; COPY FROM streaming. |
| **P92** | MBTA Communities Act 3A geographies | Different source (DHCD ArcGIS portal, not MassGIS). |
| **P93** | Bulk-flip remaining 11 P1 layer configs | Each is a one-line change + cohort import. Should be ~1 day total. |
| **P94** | Frontend "data layer" tab | Show `/api/v1/spatial/layers` in the existing mapviewer to switch from MassGIS WMS → local PostGIS rendering after layer is imported. |
| **P95** | DEM / LiDAR raster | 200GB; separate raster pipeline (raster2pgsql + tile cache). |

---

## Known boundaries

1. **No file persistence** — raw WFS payloads aren't stored on disk. To re-audit a feature you replay the WFS call. If MassGIS ever takes a layer offline, we lose audit ability. Acceptable trade-off (saves 50GB+ disk; layers are public + versioned upstream).
2. **No incremental sync** — ETL is full fresh-load each run. For high-cadence layers (FEMA NFHL changes when town gets new study) we'll want delta-aware sync; defer to a future P-item if it becomes a problem.
3. **Single-geometry-type per layer** — a `Polygon` source feature reaching a `polygon` target gets coerced to `MultiPolygon`. Mixed-geom-type layers (e.g. some MassGIS layers expose both Polygon and Point in one collection) would need a future enhancement.
4. **No layer authentication** — all MassGIS WFS layers are public. Layers behind auth (e.g. some BM3 internal sub-products) need a `WFSSource(... auth=...)` extension.
5. **Schema hand-written for `regulatory_overlay.metadata` values** — when a P-item adds a new layer, the `keep_attrs` tuple is the only place schema is documented for that layer's JSONB. No JSON Schema enforcement on insert.

# PlotMax 文档体系索引

> PlotMax — 美国 Zoning Code 智能分析与地块开发潜力评估平台

*最后更新: 2026-04-13（v1.7.0 状态同步 + 新增 REQ-17 Zoning Map PRD）*

---

## 🗂 全局状态

👉 **[STATUS.md](./STATUS.md)** — 跨 PRD 实施状态总览（v1.7.0）

---

## 📋 需求文档（Requirements）

| 编号 | 文件 | 内容 | 状态 |
|------|------|------|------|
| REQ-01 | `01_PRD_产品需求文档.md` | 产品愿景、用户画像、核心功能清单、MVP 范围定义 | 🔵 进行中 |
| REQ-02 | `02_JDSR_数据源注册系统需求.md` | Jurisdiction Data Source Registry — 数据模型、发现策略、zoning_code_normalization 表 | ✅ 主体完成 |
| REQ-03 | `03_CRAWLER_法规爬取引擎需求.md` | 多策略爬虫引擎 — 5 种 crawler + 新增 MapImageCrawler（REQ-17 Layer 3 输入）| ✅ 文本爬虫完成 / 🔲 Map 爬虫未开始 |
| REQ-04 | `04_POLICY_政策解析引擎需求.md` | LLM 结构化提取 — Policy Schema、3-Pass 策略、Zoning Code Normalization 候选生成 | ✅ 主体完成 |
| REQ-05 | `05_PARCEL_地块数据与空间分析需求.md` | MassGIS Parcel、Multi-Layer Cascading Spatial Join、约束叠加、可建包络 | ✅ 主体完成 / 🔲 Multi-Layer 未开始 |
| REQ-06 | `06_CALC_计算引擎需求.md` | 规则引擎、3 计算插件（Coverage/FAR/Density）、冲突解决 | ✅ 已完成 |
| REQ-07 | `07_REPORT_报告生成需求.md` | PDF 报告结构、审计追溯、模板引擎 | 🔲 未开始 |
| REQ-08 | `08_DASHBOARD_管理后台需求.md` | Web Dashboard 页面规格、技术栈、API 端点、SSE | ✅ 6 页面完成 |
| REQ-09 | `09_PIPELINE_管线任务管理需求.md` | 异步任务管理、Job 生命周期、Celery 集成 | ✅ 主体完成 |
| REQ-14 | `14_WEB_ADMIN_需求框架.md` | Web 管理后台需求框架 | 📄 参考文档 |
| REQ-15 | `15_REGULATORY_INTELLIGENCE_监管情报层扩充规划.md` | Town Bylaw 总纲要 × 叠加层 × LLM 投资分析 + REQ-17 协同 | 🔵 进行中 |
| REQ-16 | `16_CRAWLER_LIFECYCLE_DASHBOARD_爬虫生命周期可视化需求.md` | 爬虫生命周期可视化 | 🔵 部分 |
| **REQ-17** ⭐ | **`17_ZONING_MAP_空间数据获取需求.md`** | **Zoning Map 4 层获取方案：MAPC + MassGIS REST + CV Pipeline + Label Studio** | 🔲 **未开始（Phase 1b+1c）** |

## 💡 架构与决策文档（Architecture & Rationale）

| 编号 | 文件 | 内容 | 状态 |
|------|------|------|------|
| ARCH-01 | `10_ARCH_架构说明.md` | 系统架构总图、模块依赖、新增 cv_pipeline + arcgis_rest_client 模块 | ✅ 主体完成 |
| ARCH-02 | `11_RATIONALE_技术决策记录.md` | 5 条 ADR（MGL source_type / Camoufox / crawl_artifact 不可变 / 5 层监管情报 / col-md-8）| 📄 参考 |
| ARCH-03 | `12_ROADMAP_里程碑与路线图.md` | Phase 1a ✅ → Phase 1b → **Phase 1c（新增：Zoning Map）** → Phase 2 → Phase 3 | ✅ 已更新 |
| ARCH-04 | `13_DATA_FLOW_数据流图.md` | 端到端数据流图 + 新增 Zoning Map 采集子流 | ✅ 已更新 |

---

## 📁 归档（Archive）

- `archive/` — 2026-03-28 版本的 5 个 PRD 旧稿（已由 2026-03-30 版本取代，保留以防内容遗漏）

---

## 🔗 文档阅读路径

### 新人入门
1. `/CLAUDE.md`（项目主文档）
2. `REQ-01`（产品愿景）
3. `ARCH-01`（系统架构）
4. `STATUS.md`（当前进展）

### 技术实现
1. `REQ-02` JDSR（数据源基础）
2. `REQ-03` CRAWLER（数据采集）
3. `REQ-04` POLICY（结构化提取）
4. `REQ-17` **ZONING MAP**（空间数据获取 — 当前优先级）
5. `REQ-05` PARCEL（地块分析）
6. `REQ-06` CALC（规则计算）

### 投资分析视角
1. `REQ-15` REGULATORY INTELLIGENCE（监管情报层）
2. `REQ-17` ZONING MAP（空间数据）
3. `REQ-01` PRD（产品定位）

---

## 变更历史

| 日期 | 版本 | 变更 |
|------|------|------|
| 2026-04-13 | v1.7.0 索引 | 新增 REQ-17 Zoning Map PRD；9 篇现有 PRD 插入实施状态表 + Zoning Map 影响章节；新增 STATUS.md；归档 5 个 2026-03-28 旧版本 |
| 2026-03-30 | 初版 | 基于 implementation_plan.md + 三轮迭代讨论 + Dashboard/Pipeline 扩展 + 监管情报层扩充规划 |

docs.title: REQ-01: PlotMax 产品需求文档 (PRD)

versions.content:
# REQ-01: PlotMax 产品需求文档 (PRD)

*最后更新: 2026-04-13（v1.7.0 状态同步 + 新增 F-008.5 Zoning Map 多层获取）*

---

## 0. 实施状态 (v1.7.0)

| 模块 | 状态 | 版本 | 备注 |
|------|------|------|------|
| F-001 数据采集（爬虫层） | ✅ 已完成 | v0.9.4 | 4 种 crawler + MGL，351 城镇基本覆盖 |
| F-002 Policy Engine（LLM 提取） | ✅ 已完成 | v0.9.1 | 双轨策略 + 字段级置信度 |
| F-003 Parcel 空间分析 | ✅ 已完成 | v0.8.0 | MassGIS L3 + Spatial Join + Constraints |
| F-004 Calc Engine | ✅ 已完成 | v0.8.0 | Coverage/FAR/Density 3 插件 |
| F-005 Overlay 叠加层 | 🔵 部分 | v0.9.0 | DHCD SHI / MBTA §3A / OZ 已导入 |
| F-006 Dashboard | ✅ 已完成 | v1.5.0 | 交互式 Mapbox 覆盖率地图 |
| F-007 Auth & RBAC | ✅ 已完成 | v1.4.0 | JWT + bcrypt + 角色层级 |
| **F-008.5 Zoning Map 多层获取** | 🔲 **未开始** | - | **见 REQ-17（Phase 1b 优先级）** |
| F-009 Report 生成 | 🔲 未开始 | - | Phase 2 |
| F-010 对外 REST API | 🔲 未开始 | - | Phase 2 |

---

## ⚠️ 重要补充：F-008.5 Zoning Map 多层获取

原版 PRD 将 "Zoning 空间数据"视为 MassGIS 的 `ZONING` 字段即可解决，实际调研发现该字段覆盖率 <50%，**这是当前阻碍 Calc Engine 落地到地块级的最大技术瓶颈**。

解决方案：4 层递进式获取（Layer 1 MAPC / Layer 2 MassGIS REST / Layer 3 CV Pipeline / Layer 4 Label Studio）。详见 **REQ-17 Zoning Map 空间数据获取需求**。

Roadmap 调整：
- **Phase 1b**：Layer 1（MAPC Atlas 101 镇）+ Layer 2（MassGIS ArcGIS REST 自动发现）
- **Phase 1c**：Layer 3（CV Pipeline 栅格地图数字化）+ Layer 4（Label Studio 兜底）

---

## 1. 产品愿景

PlotMax 是一个面向美国市场的 Zoning 法规智能分析平台，为房地产开发者、规划师和投资人提供从法规采集到地块评估的全自动化服务。用户输入一个地址，即可获得该地块的 Zoning 规则、可建区域、开发指标和完整审计报告。

**一句话定位**: 输入地址 → 输出开发潜力评估报告。

**核心差异化**: 不只告诉你"这里允许建什么"（zoning bylaw），还整合"能否走捷径 / 需要付多少额外代价 / 哪个开发路径 ROI 最高"——通过五层监管情报叠加（本地 bylaw + 州法 + 州行政项目 + 联邦叠加 + 市场信号）实现全面分析。

---

## 2. 目标用户

| 用户角色 | 核心需求 | 使用频率 |
|----------|----------|----------|
| **小型开发商** | "这块地能盖多大？能盖几户？40B 能不能用？" — 快速筛选项目可行性 | 每周数十次查询 |
| **土地经纪人** | "这块地的 Zoning 允许什么用途？" — 辅助交易决策 | 每日使用 |
| **建筑师 / 规划师** | "Setback、FAR、Coverage 的具体数值？哪些 overlay 生效？" — 方案设计前查规 | 按项目使用 |
| **投资人** | "哪些地块有开发增值空间？OZ 税收优惠？40B 机会？" — 批量筛选低估地块 | 定期批量分析 |

---

## 3. 核心功能清单

### 3.1 P0 — MVP 必须交付

| ID | 功能 | 描述 | 状态 |
|----|------|------|------|
| F-001 | **地址查询** | 输入地址 + 城镇，返回地块基本信息（面积、业主、估值、现有用途） | 待实现 |
| F-002 | **Zoning 类型匹配** | 通过 Spatial Join 自动匹配地块所属 Zoning District | 待实现 |
| F-003 | **Dimensional 规则展示** | 显示该 Zoning 类型的 Setback、Height、FAR、Coverage 等量化规则 | 待实现 |
| F-004 | **基础可建指标计算** | 最大建筑基底面积、最大总建筑面积、最大居住单元数 | 待实现 |
| F-005 | **法规文本采集（多平台）** | 覆盖 Municode / ecode360 / 城镇 HTML / PDF + MA 州法 MGL | ✅ 已完成 |
| F-006 | **Policy 结构化提取** | 用 LLM 从法规文本提取 Dimensional Requirements 并结构化存储 | 待实现 |
| F-007 | **数据源注册（JDSR）** | 管理每个城镇的法规文本 URL 和空间数据 URL，含州级法规和叠加层来源 | ✅ 基础完成 |
| F-008 | **审计追溯** | 每个查询结果标注数据来源、版本、置信度 | ✅ crawl_artifact 完成 |
| F-009 | **管线任务管理** | Pipeline Job 统一管理爬取/提取/计算任务，SSE 实时推送状态 | 待实现 |
| F-010 | **Web Dashboard MVP** | 管线监控 + 覆盖率地图 + KPI 总览面板（Next.js + shadcn/ui） | 待实现 |

### 3.2 P1 — 监管情报层 + 空间链路

| ID | 功能 | 描述 | 状态 |
|----|------|------|------|
| F-101 | **General Bylaw 爬取** | 爬取城镇通用法规（包容性住房比例、影响费、停车要求、暴雨水管理等） | 规划中 |
| F-102 | **MGL 州法入库** | MGL Ch.40A/40B/40C/40R/40S/131/143 结构化提取，作为 Policy context | ✅ 爬取完成，提取待实现 |
| F-103 | **40B 可用性标注** | 接入 DHCD SHI 清单，标注每个城镇当前经济适用房比例 + 40B 是否可触发 | 规划中 |
| F-104 | **MBTA Communities §3A** | 标注 177 个 MBTA 城镇的 §3A 合规状态和高密度 by-right 区范围 | 规划中 |
| F-105 | **联邦叠加层** | 导入 OZ Census Tracts、FEMA Flood Zone、NRHP 历史名录 GIS 数据 | 规划中 |
| F-106 | **城镇监管总纲要** | 汇聚所有层数据，生成每个城镇的 `town_regulatory_profile` JSON | 规划中 |
| F-107 | **环境约束叠加** | 湿地缓冲区、洪泛区、坡度约束叠加到地块上 | 待实现 |
| F-108 | **Overlay District 处理** | 识别并应用叠加分区（Overlay District）的额外限制 | 待实现 |
| F-109 | **Use Table 提取** | 提取土地用途许可矩阵（Permitted / Special Permit / Not Allowed） | 待实现 |
| F-110 | **批量计算** | 对一个城镇的全部地块并行计算开发潜力 | 待实现 |
| F-111 | **PDF 报告生成** | 单地块评估报告，含地图、监管环境摘要、法规摘要、计算过程 | 待实现 |
| F-112 | **法规变更检测** | 定期检测法规 URL 内容是否变化，标记过时数据；订阅 MGL Session Law RSS | 待实现 |
| F-113 | **数据质量面板** | Dashboard 中展示 LLM 置信度分布、Spatial Join 匹配率、数据新鲜度 | 待实现 |
| F-114 | **城镇浏览器** | Dashboard 中按城镇浏览数据源、Policy 历史、健康检查记录、监管画像 | 待实现 |

### 3.3 P2 — 产品化阶段

| ID | 功能 | 描述 |
|----|------|------|
| F-201 | **对外 API** | RESTful API 供第三方系统调用 |
| F-202 | **Web 查询界面** | 地图 + 搜索框，交互式查看地块和计算结果（面向终端用户） |
| F-203 | **批量导出** | CSV / Excel 导出批量分析结果 |
| F-204 | **报告模板自定义** | 用户可选择报告内容和格式 |
| F-205 | **Variance/Special Permit 标注** | 标注哪些地块有已批准的豁免（数据来源：ZBA 会议记录） |
| F-206 | **开发路径评分** | 对每个（地块, use_type）组合输出可行性评分 + 预估审批周期 + ROI 影响因子 |
| F-207 | **法律修订追踪** | 自动订阅 MGL Session Law RSS，检测影响城镇 bylaw 的修正案 |

---

## 4. MVP 范围定义

**地理范围**: 麻州（Massachusetts）— MAPC 覆盖的 101 个城镇优先，逐步扩展至 351 个城镇。

**数据层级**:
- Layer 0: 地块基础 — MassGIS Property Tax Parcels（全州 351 镇）
- Layer 1: 本地 Zoning Bylaw — ✅ 已爬取 351 城镇（4 种来源全覆盖）
- Layer 1+: General Bylaw（通用法规）— 规划中
- Layer 2: MA 州法 — ✅ 已爬取 MGL 7 章（Ch.40A/40B/40C/40R/40S/131/143，共 339 节）
- Layer 3: 州行政项目 — DHCD SHI / MBTA §3A / 40R 区域（规划中）
- Layer 4: 联邦叠加 — OZ / FEMA / NRHP（规划中）

**计算指标（MVP）**:
- 最大建筑基底面积 (Max Building Footprint)
- 最大总建筑面积 (Max Gross Floor Area)
- 最大居住单元数 (Max Dwelling Units)
- 地块是否合规 (Conforming / Non-conforming)
- 40B 机会标注 / MBTA §3A 密度奖励适用性

**不做的事项**:
- 不做 CV 栅格地图数字化（推迟到长期研发）
- 不做多州支持（完全聚焦麻州）
- 不做 Web 查询界面（面向终端用户，Phase 2 做）— MVP 仅含内部管理 Dashboard
- 不做 Variance/Special Permit 历史查询（Phase 2+）

---

## 5. 非功能需求

| 类别 | 要求 |
|------|------|
| **数据新鲜度** | Zoning Bylaw 不超过 6 个月；MGL 州法每月检测；DHCD SHI 每季度更新 |
| **准确性** | LLM 提取的 Policy 数据置信度 ≥ 0.85 方可自动入库，否则进人工审核 |
| **可审计** | 每个计算结果必须可追溯到：数据来源 URL、抓取时间、LLM 置信度、计算规则版本 |
| **响应时间** | 单地块查询 < 3 秒（基于预计算的 Spatial Join 结果） |
| **存储** | 初期使用 Supabase (PostGIS)，月成本 < $60 |
| **安全** | 不存储用户个人信息，所有数据来自公开来源 |

---

## 6. 成功指标

| 指标 | 目标 | 当前状态 |
|------|------|---------|
| 城镇法规爬取覆盖数 | 351/351 | ✅ 已完成 |
| MGL 州法章节覆盖 | 7 章关键章节 | ✅ 已完成（339 节）|
| Zoning 匹配率 | ≥ 90% 的地块匹配到 Zoning District | 待实现 |
| Policy 提取准确率 | 人工抽检 ≥ 85% 正确 | 待实现 |
| 40B 可用性覆盖率 | 351/351 城镇标注 | 规划中 |
| MBTA §3A 合规状态 | 177/177 MBTA 城镇 | 规划中 |
| 端到端查询成功率 | ≥ 80% 的地址查询能返回完整计算结果 | 待实现 |
